How could I not remember that man
Who even after it was over
Looked at my limp body
As if he felt like doing it again?

prakrit: सुक्खंतवहलकद्दमघम्मविसूरंतकमढपाढीणं दिट्ठं अइट्ठउव्वं कालेण तलं तडाअस्स
english:
Summer reveals what was never seen before:
The bottom of the pond
With turtles and minnows
Struggling in the thick, dry mud and heat.

prakrit: चोरिअरअसद्धालुणि मा पुत्ति भम्मसु अंधआरम्मि अहिअअरं लक्खिज्जसि तमभरिए दीवअसिह व्व
english:
For the darker it is
The more clearly visible you will be,
Like the flame of a lamp.

prakrit: वाहित्ता पडिवअणं ण देइ रूसेइ एक्कमेक्कस्स असई कज्जेण विणा पलिप्पमाणे णईकच्छे
english:
She does not answer when spoken to
And is cross with everyone
For no other reason
Than that the bush on the river bank is burning.

prakrit: आम असइ म्ह ओसर पइव्वए ण तुह मइलिअं गोत्तं किं उण जणस्स जाअ व्व चंडिलं ता ण कामेमो
english:
You’re right, I am a loose woman
And you are virtuous.
So you stay clear
Before you tarnish your reputation.
But I at least don’t lust after a barber
Like the wife of someone we know.

prakrit: णिद्दं लहंति कहिअं सुणंति खलिअक्खरं ण जंपंति जाहि ण दिट्ठो सि तुमं ताओ च्चिअ सुहअ सुहिआओ
english:
My dear,
Only women who do not see you
Stay happy:
They sleep properly,
Hear what is said to them,
And do not muddle their words.

prakrit: वालअ तुमाइ दिण्णं कण्णे काऊण वोरसंघाडिं लज्जालुइणी वि वहू घरं गआ गामरच्छाए
english:
Down the center of the village street,
Wearing them behind her ear.

prakrit: अह सो विलक्खहिअओ मए अहव्वाइ अगहिआणुणओ परवज्जणच्चिरीहिं तुम्हेहि उवेक्खिओ णिंतो
english:
When he ran out of the house,
Dismayed at my rudely rejecting
His attempt at reconciliation,
You should have stopped him
Instead of laughing at someone else’s misfortune. 
Face to face and right in front of your rival
He begged you: “Be nice to me again.”

prakrit: जे लीणभमरभरभग्गगोच्छआ आसि णइअडुच्छंगे कालेण वंजुला पिअवअस्स ते खण्णुआ जाआ
english:
Remember those vañjula trees on the riverbank
Whose blossom was bowed by clusters of bees?
Time, dear friend, has turned them to stumps.

prakrit: खणभंगुरेण पेम्मेण माउआ दूमिअ म्हि एत्ताहे सिविणअणिहिलंभेण व दिट्ठपणट्ठेण लोअम्मि
english:
Aunt,
It pains me now to realize
How fragile a thing love is,
Like treasure beheld in a dream
That vanishes in the light of day.

prakrit: मालइकुसुमाइ कुलुंचिऊण मा जाण णिव्वुओ सिसिरो काअव्वा अज्ज वि णिग्गुणाण कुंदाण सामिद्धी
english:
Don’t think winter is content
Merely to wither the summer jasmine.
It also has to produce a mass
Of worthless winter jasmine.

prakrit: तुंगाण विसेसणिरंतराण सरसवणलद्धसोहाणं कअकज्जाण भडाण व थणाण पडणं पि रमणिज्जं
english:
Prominent and pressing close,
Decorated with fresh scars, their duty done,
Like two warriors, a woman’s breasts
Are handsome even as they collapse.

prakrit: परिमलणसुहा गरुआ अलद्धविवरा सलक्खणाहरणा थणआ कव्वालाअ व्व कस्स हिअए ण लग्गंति
english:
Who is not captivated by a woman’s breasts,
That, like a good poem,
Are a pleasure to grasp,
Are weighty, compact, and nicely ornamented?

prakrit: खिप्पइ हारो थणमंडलाहि तरुणीहि रमणपरिरंभे अच्चिअगुणा वि गुणिणो लहंति लहुअत्तणं काले
english:
In the act of embracing their lovers
Young ladies toss the pearl necklaces
From their round breasts.
Even the praiseworthy
Sometimes get treated with disrespect.

prakrit: अण्णो को वि सहावो वम्महसिहिणो हला हआसस्स विज्झाइ णीरसाणं हिअए सरसाण पज्जलइ
english:
Is an odd sort of fire:
In a sapless heart it goes out,
In a sappy one it instantly flares up.

prakrit: तह तस्स माणपरिवड्ढिअस्स चिरपणअवद्धमूलस्स मामि पडंतस्स सुओ सद्दो वि ण पेम्मरुक्खस्स
english:
Aunt,
When the tree of love,
Rooted in long affection,
Is struck by the axe of pride
It falls without a sound.

prakrit: पाअपडिओ ण गणिओ पिअं भणंतो वि अप्पिअं भणिओ वच्चंतो वि ण रुद्धो भण कस्स कए कओ माणो
english:
When he spoke affectionately
You answered curtly,
When he went away
You did not try to stop him.
Tell me,
What exactly do you hope to gain
By your pride?

prakrit: पुसइ खणं धुअइ खणं पप्फोडइ तक्खणं अआणंती मुद्धवहू थणवट्टे दिण्णं दइएण णक्खवअं
english:
One moment she tried wiping,
The next scrubbing,
And after that rubbing.
The young wife was completely at a loss
What to do with the nail mark
Her husband had left on her breast.

prakrit: वासारत्ते उण्णअपओहरे जोव्वणे व्व वोलीणे पढमेक्ककासकुसुमं दीसइ पलिअं व धरणीए
english:
After the rainy season
With towering clouds,
Like youth with plump breasts,
Has spent itself,
Plumes of ka\óa grass,
Like the first strands of gray hair,
Appear on Mother Earth.

prakrit: अविरलपडंतणवजलधारारज्जुघडिअं पअत्तेण अपहुत्तो उक्खिविउं रसइ व मेहो महिं उअह
english:
Hear how that cloud groans with the effort,
Yet is unable to lift the earth
With ropes of raindrops
That fall in unbroken streams.

prakrit: ओ हिअअ ओहिदिअहं तइआ पडिवज्जिऊण दइअस्स अत्थक्काउल वीसंभघाइ किं तइ समाढत्तं
english:
When my lover mentioned the date of his return
You did not object.
So what are you doing now
Destroying our belief in his early arrival?

prakrit: जो वि ण आणइ तस्स वि कहेइ भग्गाइ तेण वलआइं अइउज्जुआ वराई अहव पिओ से हआसाए
english:
Even to someone who knows nothing about it
She tells who it is who has broken her bracelets.
Either the poor girl is naïve
Or she really loves the man who has made her so
unhappy.

prakrit: सेउल्लिअसव्वंगी गोत्तग्गहणेण तस्स सुहअस्स दूइं अप्पाहेंती तस्सेअ घरंगणं पत्ता
english:
Sweating all over
From just mentioning her lover’s name,
She was so busy instructing the messenger
That she did not notice
They had arrived in front of his house.

prakrit: जम्मंतरे वि चलणे जीएणं मअण तुज्झ अच्चिस्सं जइ तं पि तेण वाणेण विज्झसे जेण हं विद्धा
english:
If you promise to pierce him
With the same arrow
As you pierced me with.

prakrit: जाव ण कोसविआसं पावइ ईसीसि मालईकलिआ मअरंदपाणलोहिल्ल भमर ताव च्चिअ मलेसि
english:
O bee,
To get at the jasmine’s nectar
You have to knead and squeeze long and hard.
It does not open by itself.

prakrit: अकअण्णुअ तुज्झ कए पाउसराईसु जं मए खुण्णं उप्पेक्खामि अलज्जिर अज्ज वि तं गामचिक्खिल्लं
english:
Even now I can see
The mud in that wretched village,
Which I squelched through on those dark nights
In the rainy season
Just to please you.
And what did I get out of it,
You shameless man?

prakrit: रेहइ गलंतकेसक्खलंतकुंडलललंतहारलआ अद्धुप्पइआ विज्जाहरि व्व पुरिसाइरी वाला
english:
Her earrings swinging
And her pearl necklace hovering,
Looks just like a vidya\dharê
Who is about to fly away.

prakrit: गामणिणो सव्वासु वि पिआसु अणुमरणगहिअवेसासु मम्मच्छेएसु वि वल्लहाइ उवरिं वलइ दिट्ठी
english:
All the village headman’s wives
Are dressed in readiness to follow him on the pyre,
Yet even at this heartrending moment
His eyes fall upon his favorite.

prakrit: मामि सरिसक्खराण वि अत्थि विसेसो पअंपिअव्वाणं णेहमइआणम् अण्णो अण्णो उवरोहमइआणं
english:
Aunt,
The words may be the same
But they sound quite different
Depending on whether they are said with affection
Or with reserve.

prakrit: हिअआहिंतो पसरंति जाइ अण्णाइ ताइ वअणाइ ओसरसु किं इमेहिं अहरंतरमेत्तभणिएहिं
english:
Words which come from the heart
Sound different.
Get away with you!
I’ve no time for words
That pay mere lip service.

prakrit: कह सा सोहग्गगुणं मए समं वहइ णिग्घिण तुमम्मि जीअ हरिज्जइ णामं हरिऊण अ दिज्जए मज्झ
english:
Have you no feelings?
How can she enjoy your favor
At the same time as me
When you rob her of her name
And give it to me?

prakrit: सहि साहसु सब्भावेण पुच्छिमो किं असेसमहिलाणं वड्ढंति करत्थ च्चिअ वलआ दइए पउत्थम्मि
english:
In your absence, cruel man,
She is as helpless as a bird in the hands of a fowler
And is only kept alive by her understanding friends. 
Friend, tell me honestly, I beg of you,
Is it the case with all women
That the bracelets grow larger on their wrists
When their husband is away?

prakrit: भमइ परिदो विसूरइ उक्खिविउं से करं पसारेइ करिणो पंकक्खुत्तस्स णेहणिअलाविआ करिणी
english:
Fettered by affection
The she-elephant circles around in distress
And stretches out her trunk to rescue
Her husband who is stuck in the mud.

prakrit: रइकेलिहिअणिअंसणकरकिसलअरुद्धणअणजुअलस्स रुद्दस्स तइअणअणं पव्वइपरिउंविअं जअइ
english:
Long live S: iva’s third eye
Which Pa\rvatê covered with kisses
While covering his other two eyes with her hands
When her skirt slipped off in the heat of love play.

prakrit: धावइ पुरओ पासेसु भमइ दिट्ठीपहम्मि संठाइ णवलइकरस्स तुह हलिअउत्त दे पहरसु वराइं
english:
As soon as you have a supple twig in your hand
She runs across your path,
Hovers on either side of you,
And happens to be
Wherever you are looking.
You blockhead,
Can’t you see the poor girl
Desperately wants a good thrashing?

prakrit: पाअडिअं सोहग्गं तंवाए उअह गोट्ठमज्झम्मि दुट्ठवुसहस्स सिंगे अच्छिउडं कंडुअंतीए
english:
See how the young cow makes plain her favored position:
In the middle of the pen
She rubs her eyelid
Against the horn of the vicious bull.

prakrit: उअ संभमविक्खित्तं रमिअव्वअलंपडाइ असईए णवरंगअं कुडंगे धअं व दिण्णं अविणअस्स
english:
Look!
The freshly dyed skirt
That the woman hurriedly kicked off
In her desire to make love
Hangs over the bower
Like a banner proclaiming her immodesty.

prakrit: हत्थप्फंसेण जरग्गवी वि पण्हअइ दोहअगुणेण अवलोइअपण्हुइरिं पुत्तअ पुण्णेहि पाविहिसि
english:
Even an old cow gives milk
At the touch of an expert milker.
My son,
You’ll have a hard time finding one
That gives milk
Merely at a glance.

prakrit: मसिणं चंकम्मंती पए पए कुणइ कीस मुहभंगं णूणं से मेहलिआ जहणगअं छिवइ णहवंतिं
english:
She walks about quite smoothly
So why pull a face at every step?
It must be her girdle
Chafing against the nail marks on her hip. 
Pretending to smear on a salve,
The young girl with delicate fingers
Applies a white bandage to her wounded lip.

prakrit: पाअपडणाण मुद्धे रहसवलामोडिचुंविअव्वाणं दंसणमेत्तपसण्णे चुक्का सि सुहाण वहुआणं
english:
Stupid woman,
Because you stopped being angry
The moment he stood before you
You missed a lot of fun:
He would have had to fall at your feet
And cover you with hot, impetuous kisses.

prakrit: णिक्खंधदुरारोहं पुत्तअ मा पाडलिं समारुहसु आरूढणिवडिआ के इमीअ ण कआ हआसाए
english:
My son,
Don’t try to climb this trumpetflower tree:
It has no branches to hold on to.
Name me a man who did not fall
When trying to climb this wretched tree.

prakrit: गामणिघरम्मि अत्ता एक्क च्चिअ पाडला इहग्गामे वहुपाडलं च सीसं दिअरस्स ण सुंदरं एअं
english:
Is at the headman’s house,
Yet my brother-in-law’s head
Is covered with trumpetflowers.
How strange!

prakrit: अण्णाण वि होंति मुहे पम्हलधवलाइ दीहकसणाइ णअणाइ सुंदरीणं तह वि हु दट्ठुं ण आणंति
english:
Long-lashed, dazzling white, black and slanting
—There are plenty of other women with eyes like that
But that does not mean they know how to look.

prakrit: दुग्गअघरम्मि घरिणी रक्खंती आउलत्तणं पइणो पुच्छिअदोहलसद्धा पुणो वि उअअं चिअ कहेइ
english:
Every time her poor in-laws ask her
What she would most like to eat,
The pregnant wife,
Anxious not to cause embarrassment to her husband,
Simply says: “Water.”

prakrit: आअंवलोअणाणं ओल्लंसुअपाअडोरुजहणाणं अवरण्हमज्जिरीणं कएण कामो वहइ चावं
english:
Try though she would to keep it,
The advice given by her friends
Escaped from her heart
That was shattered by the arrow of the God of Love. 
Returning from the late afternoon bath,
Their eyes red
And their buttocks visible through wet skirts
—Love need not carry a bow
On behalf of these women.

prakrit: विरहेण मंदरेण व हिअअं दुद्धोअहिं व महिऊण उम्मूलिआइ अव्वो अम्हं रअणाइ व सुहाइं
english:
Stirring the Ocean of Milk, which is my heart,
With the churning stick of separation
You have rooted out the pearls of my joys.

prakrit: उज्जुअरए ण तूसइ वंकम्मि वि आअमं विअप्पेइ एत्थ अहव्वाइ मए पिए पिअं कह णु काअव्वं
english:
Straightforward pleasure doesn’t satisfy him, he says,
And if I spice it up,
He wonders who taught me that.
As I always get it wrong
How will I ever make him happy?

prakrit: वहुविहविलासरसिए सुरए महिलाण को उवज्झाओ सिक्खइ असिक्खिआइ वि सव्वो णेहाणुवंधेण
english:
In lovemaking, with its manifold and intricate delights,
Who is there to instruct women?
One can learn anything
Simply by being shown some affection.

prakrit: वण्णवसिए विअत्थसि सच्चं चिअ सो तुए ण संभविओ ण हु होंति तम्मि दिट्ठे सुत्थावत्थाइ अंगाइं
english:
You look just as you did yesterday,
So you must be bragging.
In fact you did not see him,
For if you had,
You would no longer look so well.

prakrit: आसण्णविवाहदिणे अहिणववहुसंगमुस्सुअमणस्स पढमघरिणीअ सुरअं वरस्स हिअए ण संठाइ
english:
As the wedding day draws near
He can think only of making love
With his new bride.
The pleasures once shared
With his first wife
No longer find a place in his heart.

prakrit: जइ लोअणिंदिअं जइ अमंगलं जइ वि मुक्कमज्जाअं पुप्फवइदंसणं तह वि देइ हिअअस्स णिव्वाणं
english:
Though people condemn it,
Though it is highly inauspicious
And considered most improper,
The sight of a woman during her period
Fills the heart with heavenly bliss.

prakrit: जइ ण छिवसि पुप्फवइं पुरओ ता कीस वारिओ ठासि छित्तो सि चुलचुलंतेहि धाविऊण ăम्ह हत्थेहि
english:
If you do not want to touch a woman during her period,
Why get in my way when you shouldn’t?
I will touch you with my itchy hands
As I push past you.

prakrit: ण वि तह अइगरुएण वि तम्मइ हिअए भरेण गब्भस्स जह विवरीअणिहुअणं पिअम्मि सोण्हा अपावंती
english:
Our daughter-in-law is not so much distressed
By the very heavy weight in her womb
As by the fact that due to it
She can no longer make love
On top of her husband.

prakrit: अगणिअजणाववाअं अवहत्थिअगुरुअणं वराईए तुह गलिअदंसणासाइ तीअ वलिउं चिरं रुण्णं
english:
Not caring what people might say
And pushing her parents aside,
The poor girl
With no hope of ever seeing you again,
Lay rolling on the ground crying her heart out.

prakrit: हिअअं हिअए णिहिअं चित्तालिहिअ व्व तुह मुहे दिट्ठी आलिंगणरहिआइं णवरं झिज्जंति अंगाइं
english:
My heart is lodged in your heart,
My eyes, like eyes in a painting,
Are immovably fixed on your face,
It is only my body
That is wasting away
For lack of your embrace.

prakrit: अहअं विओअतणुई दुसहो विरहाणलो चलं जीअं अप्पाहिज्जउ किं सहि जाणसि तं चेअ जं जुत्तं
english:
“My life hangs in the balance.”
Why am I telling you this, my friend?
You know yourself the sort of thing that needs saying.

prakrit: तुह विरहुज्जागरओ सिविणे वि ण देइ दंसणसुहाइ वाहेण पहालोअणविणोअणं से हअं तं पि
english:
As she can’t sleep when you are away
She is deprived of the pleasure of seeing you in her dreams,
And her tears spoil the pastime of looking out for you.

prakrit: अण्णावराहकुविओ जह तह कालेण गच्छइ पसाअं वेसत्तणावराहे कुविअं कह तं पसाएमि
english:
A man who is angry at one’s being bossy
Can be won round in due course,
But someone who is angry at one’s being servile
How can I appease him?

prakrit: दीससि पिआइ जंपसि सब्भावो सुहअ एत्तिओ च्चेअ फालेइऊण हिअअं साहसु को दावए कस्स
english:
You allow me to see you
And still speak kindly.
But, my dear, is this
The way to behave?
For who would break a heart
And having broken it
Still show his face?

prakrit: उअअं लहिउं उत्ताणिआणणा होंति के वि सविसेसं रित्ता णमंति सुइरं रहट्टघडिअ व्व कापुरिसा
english:
The bow shoots the arrow
The moment it touches the string.
How long can straight and crooked stay together? 
When full of water, they swing high.
When empty, they lie low for a while.
—The buckets on a water wheel
Behave just like nasty people.

prakrit: भग्गपिअसंगमं केत्तिअं व जोण्हाजलं णहसरम्मि चंदअरपणालणिझरणिवहपडंतं ण णिट्ठाइ
english:
How easy it is for the moon
To spoil my nightly assignations!
For there is no end to the cascade of moonbeams
With which it empties itself into the lake of the sky.

prakrit: अइकोवणा वि सासू रुआविआ गअवईअ सोण्हाए पाअपडनोणआए दोसु वि गलिएसु वलएसु
english:
Slipped off as she fell at her feet,
So long had her husband been abroad.

prakrit: रोवंति व्व अरण्णे दूसहरइकिरिणफंससंतत्ता अइतारझिल्लिविरुएहि पाअवा गिम्हमज्झण्हे
english:
At noon in summer
The trees in the deserted forest,
Scorched by the unbearable rays of the sun,
Seem to cry out with the shrill creak
Of crickets.

prakrit: पढमणिलीणमहुरमहुलोहिल्लालिउलवद्धझंकारं अहिमअरकिरणणिउरुंवचुंविअं दलइ कमलवणं
english:
Bees settle on it,
Buzzing wildly,
Lusting for its sweet nectar
But the lotus opens
Only after being kissed by the sun.

prakrit: गोत्तक्खलणं सोऊण पिअअमे अज्ज तीअ छणदिअहे वज्झमहिसस्स माल व्व मंडणं उअह पडिहाइ
english:
Just look!
After her husband had addressed her with his mistress’s name
The ornaments she had put on for the fair
Suddenly appeared like a garland on the head of a buffalo
Being led to sacrifice.

prakrit: महमहइ मलअवाओ अत्ता वारेइ मं घरा णिंतिं अंकोल्लपरिमलेण वि जो क्खु मुओ सो मुओ च्चेअ
english:
The south wind blows strong,
Mother-in-law keeps me locked up indoors,
But someone who dies sniffing the scent of am≥kolla
Dies all the same.

prakrit: मुहपेच्छओ पई से सा वि हु सविसेसदंसणुम्मइआ दो वि कअत्था पुहइं अमहिलपुरिसं व मण्णंति
english:
His eyes are glued to her face,
And she is intoxicated at the sight of him.
So utterly content are these two,
There might not be another man or woman on earth.

prakrit: खेमं कत्तो खेमं जो सो खुज्जंवओ घरद्दारे तस्स किर मत्थआओ को वि अणत्थो समुप्पण्णो
english:
Well? What do you mean, “Are you well?”
That stunted mango by the front door—
They tell me something nasty is coming out of
its top.

prakrit: आउच्छणविच्छाअं जाआइ मुहं णिअच्छमाणेण पहिएण सोअणिअलाविएण गंतुं चिअ ण इट्ठं
english:
Seeing his wife’s face grow pale
As he bade her farewell
The husband could not bring himself to leave,
His feet fettered by affection.

prakrit: सूईवेहे मुसलं विच्छुहमाणेण डड्ढलोएण एक्कग्गामे वि पिओ समेहि अच्छीहि वि ण दिट्ठो
english:
I dare not set eyes on my lover
Though we live in the same village.

prakrit: अज्जं पि दाव एक्कं मा मं वारेहि पिअसहि रुअंतिं कल्लिं उण तम्मि गए जइ ण मुआ ता ण रोइस्सं
english:
Dear friend,
Allow me this one day of crying.
Tomorrow, I promise,
When he has left
And if I’m still alive,
I will cry no more.

prakrit: एहि त्ति वाहरंतम्मि पिअअमे उअह ओणअमुहीए विउणावेढिअजहणत्थलाइ लज्जोणअं हसिअं
english:
And, with an embarrassed laugh,
Wrapped her skirt twice around her thighs.

prakrit: तुह दंसणे सअण्हा सद्दं सोऊण णिग्गआ जाइं तइ वोलीणे ताइं पआइ वोढव्वआ जाआ
english:
At the sound of your voice
She rushed out of the house
In her desire to see you.
Once you had passed
She had to be carried back again.

prakrit: ईसामच्छररहिए हि णिव्विआरेहि मामि अच्छीहिं एण्हिं जणो जणम् मिव णिअच्छए कुह ण झिज्जामो
english:
Without a trace of jealousy or anger,
As if I were just anybody.
So how can I help growing thin?

prakrit: वाउद्धअसिचअविहाविओरुदिट्ठेण दंतमग्गेण वहुमाआ तोसिज्जइ णिहाणकलसस्स व मुहेण
english:
When the wind tossed up the wife’s skirt,
Exposing the tooth marks on her thighs,
Her mother was thrilled,
As if she had hit upon the lid of a pot of gold.

prakrit: हिअअम्मि वससि ण करेसि मण्णुअं तह वि णेहभरिएहिं संकिज्जसि जुअइसहावगलिअधीरेहि अम्हेहिं
english:
You abide in my heart
And never trouble it.
Yet, for all my affection,
I lose faith, as girls do,
And grow suspicious.

prakrit: अण्णं पि किं पि पाविहिसि मूढ मा तम्म दुक्खमेत्तेण हिअअ पराहीणजणं महंत तुह केत्तिअं एअं
english:
Foolish heart,
Why so distressed?
Worse will befall you,
Running after a man
In thrall to another.

prakrit: वेसो सि जीअ पंसुल अहिअअरं सा हु वल्लहा तुज्झ इअ जाणिऊण वि मए ण ईसिअं डड्ढपेम्मस्स
english:
How wayward of you:
The more a woman detests you
The more you like her.
Though I know this all too well
There is nothing I can do
About this cursed love of mine.

prakrit: सा आम सुहअ गुणरूअसोहिरी आम णिग्गुणा अहअं भण तीअ जो ण सरिसो किं सो सव्वो जणो मरउ
english:
I agree, my dear,
She is a model of virtue and beauty,
And, yes,
I am quite worthless.
But, tell me,
Must everyone not up to her standard
Be condemned to death?

prakrit: संतम् असंतं दुक्खम् सुहं च जाओ घरस्स जाणंति ता पुत्तअ महिलाओ सेसाउ जरा मणुस्साणं
english:
Those who know what is good for the household
And what isn’t,
Know what it can put up with
And what it can’t
—Those are proper wives.
The rest are a drain on men.

prakrit: हसिएहि उवालंभा अच्चुवआरेहि खिज्जिअव्वाइं अंसूहि भंडणाइं एसो मग्गो सुमहिलाणं
english:
Instead of criticism smiles,
Instead of grievances extreme politeness,
Instead of quarrels tears,
Such is the way of good wives.

prakrit: उल्लावो मा दिज्जउ लोअविरुद्धं ति णाम काऊण समुहावडिए को उण वेसे दिट्ठिं ण पाडेइ
english:
We should not be seen talking
Because people disapprove.
But how can one help setting eyes
Even on a person one detests
If he happens to cross one’s path?

prakrit: किं रुअसि किं व सोअसि किं कुप्पसि सुअणु एक्कमेक्कस्स पेम्मं विसं व विसमं साहसु को रुंभिउं तरइ
english:
Why cry, why lament,
Why, slender girl,
Get angry with each and every one?
Love is a lethal poison.
And, tell me,
Can anyone withstand it?

prakrit: ते अ जुआणा ता गामसंपआ तं च अम्ह तारुण्णं अक्खाणअं व लोओ कहेइ अम्हे वि तं सुणिमो
english:
There were the young men,
The blessings of village life,
And the days of our youth.
Now people make a story of it,
And we listen.

prakrit: वाहोहभरिअगंडाहराइ भणिअं विलक्खहसिरीए अज्ज वि किं रूसिज्जइ सवहावत्थं गअं पेम्मं
english:
Tears were still running down her cheeks and lips
When, with a shy laugh, she said:
“But why am I still angry
When our love has reached the stage of swearing
fidelity?”

prakrit: वण्णग्घअतुप्पमुहिं जो मं अइआअरेण चुंवंतो एण्हिं सो भूसणभूसिअं पि अलसाअइ छिवंतो
english:
The man who kissed me with such extraordinary care
When my face was smeared with red ghee
Now hardly cares even to touch me
Despite my wearing this lovely jewelry.

prakrit: णीलवडपाउअंगि त्ति मा हु णं परिहरिज्जासु पट्टंसुअं पि णद्धं रअम्मि अवणिज्जइ च्चेअ
english:
That she has come all swathed in black
Shouldn’t deter you.
Even a tight breast band
Is prized off in the heat of love.

prakrit: सच्चं कलहे कलहे सुरआरंभा पुणो णवा होंति माणो उण माणंसिणि गरुओ पेम्मं विणासेइ
english:
After every quarrel, it’s true,
The pleasures of love taste new.
But, proud woman,
Too much of this pride
And his love will be destroyed.

prakrit: लज्जा चत्ता सीलं च खंडिअं अजसघोसणा दिण्णा जस्स कएणं पिअसहि सो च्चेअ जणो जणो जाओ
english:
Dear friend,
The man for whom I abandoned modesty,
Destroyed my character
And lost my good name
Now treats me just like anyone else.

prakrit: हसिअं अइट्ठदंतं भमिअम् अणिक्कंतदेहलीदेसं दिट्टम् अणुक्खित्तमुहं एसो मग्गो कुलवहूणं
english:
Looking without raising her face:
Such is the way of a virtuous wife.

prakrit: धूलिमइलो वि पंकंकिओ वि तणरइअदेहभरणो वि तह वि गइंदो गरुअत्तणेण ढक्कं समुव्वहइ
english:
Soiled with dust,
Spattered with mud
And nourished with grass,
Yet, on account of his size,
It is the elephant who carries the big drum.

prakrit: करमरि कीस ण गम्मइ को गव्वो जेण मसिणगमणा सि अदिट्ठदंतं हसिरीअ जंपिअं चोर जाणिहिसि
english:
Why aren’t you trying to escape?
What makes you so confident,
That you remain calm?
With a smile the bowman’s wife replies:
“You will soon find out, kidnapper!”

prakrit: थोरंसुएहि रुण्णं सवत्तिवग्गेण पुप्फवइआए भुअसिहरं पइणो पेच्छिऊण सिरलग्गतुप्पलिअं
english:
From the youngest wife’s face
On the husband’s shoulder.

prakrit: लोओ जूरइ जूरउ वअणिज्जं होइ होउ तं णाम एहि णिमज्जसु पासे पुप्फवइ ण एइ मे णिद्दा
english:
And snuggle down beside me,
For I can get no sleep.

prakrit: जं जं पुलएमि दिसं पुरओ लिहिओ व्व ईससे तत्थ तुह पडिमापरिवाडिं वहइ व सअलं दिसाचक्कं
english:
In whatever direction I gaze I see your image before me.
The whole horizon is like a panorama with paintings of you
alone.

prakrit: ओसरइ धुणइ साहं खोक्खामुहलो पुणो समुल्लिहइ जंवूफलं ण गेण्हइ भमरो त्ति कई पढमडक्को
english:
The monkey scratches the rose apple with its finger,
Jumps away, shakes the branch, screeching loudly,
But does not dare to pluck it
As it looks like the bee
That once stung him.

prakrit: उप्पहपहाविअजणो पविअंभिअकलअलो पहअतूरो अव्वो सो च्चेअ छणो तेण विणा गामडाहो व्व
english:
Loud beating of drums:
The normal bustle of a festival
But oh! without him
More like a village fire.

prakrit: असमत्तगरुअकज्जे एण्हिं पहिए घरं णिअत्तंते णवपाउसो पिउच्छा हसइ व कुडअट्टहासेहिं
english:
Aunt,
As the traveler hastens homewards,
His business unfinished,
The start of the rainy season,
With its white kuèaja flowers,
Seems to mock him with loud laughter.

prakrit: दट्ठूण उण्णमंते मेहे आमुक्कजीविआसाए पहिअघरिणीअ डिंभो ओरुण्णमुहीअ सच्चविओ
english:
Seeing clouds piling up in the west
The traveler’s wife,
Who had despaired of her life,
Began to decorate her body
While tears streamed down her face.

prakrit: अविहवलक्खणवलअं ठाणं णेंतो पुणो पुणो गलिअं सहिसत्थो च्चिअ माणंसिणीअ वलआरओ जाओ
english:
To make sure she didn’t look like a widow
Her friends constantly hitched up
The sulking girl’s fallen bangle
As though they were bangle sellers.

prakrit: पहिअवहू विवरंतरगलिअजलोल्ले घरे अणोल्लं पि उद्देसं अविरअवाहसलिलणिवहेण ओल्लेइ
english:
While rain trickled down
Through gaps in the thatch
The traveler’s wife wept
And with her ceaseless flow of tears
Soaked the last dry spot in the house.

prakrit: दीसइ ण चूअमउलं अत्ता ण अ वाइ मलअगंधवहो पत्तं वसंतमासं साहइ उक्कंठिअं चेअ
english:
Yet the yearning in my heart
Tells me that spring is on its way.

prakrit: अंववणे भमरउलं ण विणा कज्जेण ऊसुअं भमइ कत्तो जलणेण विणा धूमस्स सिहाउ दीसंति
english:
Not without reason
Do the bees hover eagerly
Over the grove of mangoes.
There is no smoke without fire.

prakrit: दइअकरग्गहलुलिओ धम्मिल्लो सीहुगंधिअं वअणं मअणम्मि एत्तिअं चिअ पसाहणं हरइ तरुणीणं
english:
Loose-hanging hair
To take in one’s hands
And a mouth smelling of wine:
That’s all young women need
To bring with them
As allurement in love.

prakrit: गामतरुणीउ हिअअं हरंति छेआण थणहरिल्लीओ मअणे कुसुंभराइल्लकंचुआभरणमेत्ताओ
english:
Village girls with swelling bosoms
Need only simple saffron yellow blouses
To attract the most sophisticated of lovers.

prakrit: आलोअंत दिसाओ ससंत जंभंत गंत रोअंत मुच्छंत पडंत खलंत पहिअ किं ते पउत्थेण
english:
Traveler,
Why did you ever leave home
If all you do is scan the horizon,
Sigh, yawn, moan, groan,
Faint, trip and stumble?

prakrit: दट्ठूण तरुणसुरअं विविहविलासेहि करणसोहिल्लं दीवो वि तग्गअमणो गअं पि तेल्लं ण लक्खेइ
english:
The lamp was so absorbed
In looking at the young couple
Playfully making love
In all sorts of different positions
That it did not notice that the oil had run out.

prakrit: पुणरुत्तकरप्फालणम् उहअतडुल्लिहणपीडणसआइं जूहाहिवस्स माए पुणो वि जइ णम्मआ सहइ
english:
Mother,
What’s it to me
If the Narmada\ lets the elephant bull
Beat her again and again with his trunk
And scrape and squeeze both her banks?

prakrit: वोडसुणओ विवण्णो अत्ता मत्ता पई वि अण्णत्थो फडही वि मोडिआ महिसएण को कस्स साहेउ
english:
The nasty dog is dead,
Mother-in-law is drunk,
My husband is out,
And the buffalo has broken the bolt.
Who is going to tell on us?

prakrit: गिरिसोत्तो त्ति भुअंगं महिसो जीहाइ लिहइ संतत्तो महिसस्स कण्हपत्थरझरो त्ति सप्पो पिअइ लालं
english:
Parched by the heat, the buffalo licks a snake,
Taking it for a mountain rivulet,
And the snake sips the buffalo’s spittle,
Taking it for water falling from black rock.

prakrit: पत्तणिअंवप्फंसा ण्हाणुत्तिण्णाइ सामलंगीए जलविंदुएहि चिहुरा रुअंति वंधस्स व भएण
english:
When the lovely woman had finished her bath
Her hair,
Which had known the pleasure
Of touching her buttocks,
Started to weep drops of water
At the thought of being tied into a bun again.

prakrit: सुप्पं डड्ढं चणआ ण भज्जिआ सो जुआ अइक्कंतो अत्ता वि घरे कुविआ भूआण व वाइओ वंसो
english:
The soup is burnt,
The chickpeas aren’t roasted,
The young man has gone past,
And I am stuck in this house
With an angry mother-in-law.
It’s like playing the flute to phantoms.

prakrit: पिसुणेंति कामिणीणं जललुक्कपिआवऊहणसुहेल्लिं कंडइअकवोलुप्फुल्लणिच्चलच्छीइ वअणाइ
english:
By their wide and staring eyes
And the gooseflesh on their cheeks
These women betray their lovers
Who, hidden underwater,
Have put their arms around them.

prakrit: महिसक्खंधविलग्गं घोलइ सिंगाहअं सिमिसिमंतं आहअवीणाझंकारसद्दमुहलं मसअवुंदं
english:
A swarm of gnats hovers over the buffalo’s shoulders.
When he hits them with his horn
They hum like the plucked strings of a lute.

prakrit: रेहंति कुमुअदलणिच्चलट्ठिआ मत्तमहुअरणिहाआ ससिअरणीसेसपणासिअस्स गंठि व्व तिमिरस्स
english:
Drunken bees sit motionless
On the petals of the white lotus
Like specks of darkness
Forgotten by the rays of the moon.

prakrit: धाराधुव्वंतमुहा लंविअवक्खा णिउंचिअग्गीवा वइवेढणेसु काआ सूलाहिण्ण व्व दीसंति
english:
The crows on the boundary fence,
With their faces bedraggled, their wings drooping, their
necks sunken,
Look as though they had been impaled.

prakrit: ण वि तह अनालवंती हिअअं दूमेइ माणिणी अहिअं जह दूरविअंभिअगरुअरोसमज्झत्थभणिएहिं
english:
A sulking woman
Does not hurt so much by silence
As by small talk
After overwhelming anger.

prakrit: गंधं अग्घाअंतअ पिक्ककलंवाण वाहभरिअच्छ आससु पहिअजुआणअ घरिणिमुहं मा ण पेच्छिहिसि
english:
Sniffing the wayside kadamba blossom,
Your eyes full of tears,
Take heart, young traveler!
You are sure to see the face of your wife again.

prakrit: गज्ज महं चिअ उवरिं सव्वत्थामेण लोहहिअअस्स जलहर लंवालइअं मा रे मारेहिसि वराइं
english:
For I have a heart of iron.
But spare my poor girl at home,
Who has already loosened her hair,
As it would be death to her.

prakrit: संझाराओत्थइओ दीसइ गअणम्मि पडिवआचंदो रत्तदुऊलंतरिओ थणणहलेहो व्व णववहूए
english:
The waning moon veiled by the red sky at dawn
Looks like the nail mark on the young bride’s bosom
Showing through her crimson gauze.

prakrit: अइ दिअर किं ण पेच्छसि आआसं किं मुहा पलोएसि जाआइ वाहुमूलम्मि अद्धअंदाण परिवाडिं
english:
My dear man,
Why scan the sky
If it’s crescent moons you’re after?
Try looking at your sister-in-law’s shoulder:
You’ll find a whole row of them there.

prakrit: वाआइ किं भणिज्जउ केत्तिअमेत्तं व लिक्खए लेहे तुह विरहे जं दुक्खं तस्स तुमं चेअ गहिअत्थो
english:
How can I put it in words?
One can say so little in a letter.
But the ache I feel in your absence
Is something you know of yourself.

prakrit: मअणग्गिणो व्व धूमं मोहणपिच्छिं व लोअदिट्ठीए जोव्वणधअं व मुद्धा वहइ सुअंधं चिउरभारं
english:
Like a bunch of peacock’s tail feathers
Waved by the conjuror to distract his audience,
Like the victory banner of youth.

prakrit: रुंदारविंदमंदिरमअरंदाणंदिआलिरिंछोली झणझणइ कसणमणिमेहल व्व महुमासलच्छीए
english:
The line of bees around the large lotus flower,
Feasting loudly on its nectar,
Looks like the jingling girdle of sapphires
Around the waist of the nymph of spring.

prakrit: चोरा सभअसतण्हं पुणो पुणो पेसअंति दिट्ठीओ अहिरक्खिअणिहिकलसे व्व पोढवइआथनुच्छंगे
english:
With fear and with longing
The kidnappers repeatedly steal glances
At the round breasts of the warrior’s wife
As if they were pots of gold
Guarded by snakes.

prakrit: केत्तिअमेत्तं होहिइ सोहग्गं पिअअमस्स भमिरस्स महिलामअणछुहाउलकडक्खविक्खेवघेप्पंतं
english:
How much happiness
Can my errant husband get
From the sidelong glances
Cast by women starved of love?

prakrit: णिअधणिअं उवऊहसु कुक्कुडसद्देण झत्ति पडिवुद्धो परवसहिवाससंकिर णिअए वि घरम्मि मा भासु
english:
It’s your own wife:
You can embrace her.
Suddenly awoken by cockcrow,
You look around distraught
As if you’d spent the night
In another man’s house,
But don’t be alarmed:
It’s your own.

prakrit: णवपल्लवं विसण्णा पहिआ पेच्छंति चूअरुक्खस्स कामस्स लोहिउप्पंगराइअं हत्थभल्लं व
english:
In despair
The travelers stare at the new shoot on the mango tree,
As though it were one of Love’s arrows
Crimson with a clot of blood.

prakrit: महिलाणं चिअ दोसो जेण पवासम्मि गव्विआ पुरिसा दो तिण्णि जाव ण मरंति ता ण विरहा समप्पंति
english:
That their husbands blithely go abroad
Is the fault of their wives.
The moment one or two of them die
There will be no more talk of separation.

prakrit: वालअ दे वच्च लहुं मरइ वराई अलं विलंवेण सा तुज्झ दंसणेण वि जीवेज्ज ण एत्थ संदेहो
english:
Hey, you fool,
Can’t you walk a little faster?
The poor girl is dying.
Stop dragging your feet.
The mere sight of you will revive her.
No doubt of it.

prakrit: तंमिरपसरिअहुअवहजालोलिपलीविए वणाभोए किंसुअवणं ति कलिऊण मुद्धहरिणो ण णिक्कमइ
english:
While all around the forest is burning,
Lines of red flame steadily advancing,
The foolish doe does not flee,
Imagining herself in a forest of flame trees.

prakrit: णिहुअणसिप्पं तह सारिआइ उल्लाविअं म्ह गुरुपुरओ जह तं वेलं माए ण आणिमो कत्थ वच्चामो
english:
The mynah bird has prattled
In front of the elders
About what we get up to in bed
So that, for the moment,
I don’t know which way to turn.

prakrit: पच्चग्गुप्फुल्लदलुल्लसंतमअरंदपाणलेहलओ तं ण त्थि कुंदकलिआइ जं ण भमरो महइ काउं
english:
Eager to taste the fresh nectar
That seeps from between the petals
At the moment they unfold,
There is nothing the bee will not do
To the jasmine’s closed bud.

prakrit: सो को वि गुणाइसओ ण आणिमो मामि कुंदलइआए अच्छीहिं चिअ पाउं अहिलस्सइ जेण भमरेहिं
english:
Aunt,
There must be something very special about the early
jasmine
That we don’t know about,
Such that all the bees converge on its tiny flowers.

prakrit: एक्क च्चिअ रूअगुणं गामणिधूआ समुव्वहइ अणिमिसणअणो सअलो जीए देवीकओ गामो
english:
The village headman’s daughter is such a beauty
That she has on her own turned all the men of the village
into gods
By making them unable to blink.

prakrit: मण्णे आसाओ च्चिअ ण पाविओ पिअअमाहररसस्स तिअसेहि जेण रअणाअराहि अमअं समुद्धरिअं
english:
That they are churning the ocean
To retrieve the nectar of immortality
Only shows that the gods have not yet tasted
your lips.

prakrit: आअण्णाअड्ढिअणिसिअभल्लमम्माहआइ हरिणीए अद्दंसणो पिओ होहिइ त्ति वलिउं चिरं दित्थो
english:
Mortally wounded by a sharp arrow
Shot from behind the hunter’s ear,
The doe, knowing she would not see her mate again,
Rolled over and fixed him with a lingering gaze.

prakrit: मालारी ललिउल्लुलिअवाहुमूलेहि तरुणहिअआइं उल्लूरइ सज्जुल्लूरिआइ कुसुमाइ दावेंती
english:
Lifting up her lovely arms
To display the freshly plucked flowers,
The garland seller plucks at
The hearts of young men.

prakrit: मज्झो पिओ कुअंडो पल्लिजुआणा सवत्तीओ जह जह वड्ढंति थणा तह तह झिज्जंति पंच वाहीए
english:
The fuller the breasts of the hunter’s wife
The thinner her waist,
The thinner her loving husband and his bow,
The thinner the youths in her village,
And the thinner her husband’s other wives. 
That daughter-in-law has turned our son
Into a trembling wreck:

prakrit: मालारीए वेल्लहलवाहुमूलावलोअणसअण्हो अलिअं पि भमइ कुसुमग्घपुच्छिरो पंसुलजुआणो
english:
Eager to feast his eyes
On the flower seller’s
Gorgeous shoulders,
The young rake finds an excuse
For hanging around
By cross-questioning her
About her prices.

prakrit: मंदं पि ण आणइ हलिअणंदणो इह हि डड्ढगामम्मि गहवइसुआ विवज्जइ अवेज्जए कस्स साहामो
english:
The plowman’s son in this damned village
Hasn’t a clue.
The landowner’s daughter is dying
But with no doctor at hand
Whom to tell?

prakrit: एक्कक्कमपरिरक्खणपहारसमुहे कुरंगमिहुणम्मि वाहेण मण्णुविअलंतवाहधोअं धणुं मुक्कं
english:
As the stag steps in front of the doe
To face the arrow
And then the doe steps in front of the stag,
The hunter throws away his bow
Drenched with tears of distress.

prakrit: ता सुहअ विलंव खणं भणामि कीअ वि कएण अलम् अहवा अविआरिअकज्जारंभआरिणी मरउ ण भणिस्सं
english:
Wait a moment, my handsome friend,
I’ve a message from someone.
Or perhaps I haven’t.
She always acts so rashly,
She’d be better off dead.
No, I’ve nothing to tell you.

prakrit: भोइणिदिण्णपहेणअचक्खिअदुस्सिक्खिओ हलिअउत्तो एत्ताहे अण्णपहेणआण छीवोल्लअं देइ
english:
The plowman is so spoilt by the sweets
The tax gatherer’s wife has given him to eat
That he now turns up his nose at all other sweets.

prakrit: वाउव्वेल्लिअसाहुलि थएसु फुडदंतमंडलं जहणं चडुआरअं पइं मा हु पुत्ति जणहासिअं कुणसु
english:
You stupid girl,
Don’t make a laughingstock
Of your doting husband.

prakrit: गम्मिहिसि तस्स पासं सुंदरि मा तुअर वड्ढउ मिअंको दुद्धे दुद्धं मिव चंदिआइ को पेच्छइ मुहं दे
english:
You will meet him,
No need to hurry,
Wait till the moon is full.
For no one will tell your face
Apart from moonlight
Any more than milk from milk.

prakrit: जइ जूरइ जूरउ णाम मामि परलोअवसणिओ लोओ तह वि वला गामणिणंदणस्स वअणे वलइ दिट्ठी
english:
Aunt,
If these sanctimonious people object,
Let them.
One can’t help it if one’s eyes stray
Toward the headman’s son.

prakrit: गेहं व वित्तरहिअं णिज्झरकुहरं व सलिलसुण्णइअं गोहणरहिअं गोट्ठं व तीअ वअणं तुह विओए
english:
Like a house without furnishings,
Like the basin of a waterfall without water,
Like a cowshed without cattle
—Her face without you.

prakrit: तुह दंसणेण जणिओ इमीअ लज्जाउलाइ अणुराओ दुग्गअमणोरहो विअ हिअअ च्चिअ जाइ परिणामं
english:
This girl is so shy
That the feelings aroused in her
By the sight of you,
Like a poor man’s dreams, find
Fulfillment only in the mind.

prakrit: जं तणुआअइ सा तुह कएण किं जेण पुच्छसि हसंतो अह गिम्हे मह पअई एव्वं भणिऊण ओरुण्णा
english:
Since it is due to you
That she has grown thin
How could you jokingly ask her why?
“I usually do in summer” was her reply
Before she broke down.

prakrit: अविहत्तसंधिवंधं पढमरसुब्भेअपाणलोहिल्लो उव्वेल्लिउं ण आणइ खंडइ कलिआमुहं भमरो
english:
The bee is so eager to taste the fresh nectar,
He cannot stop to unfold the tight-knit petals
But simply pierces the tip of the bud.

prakrit: दरवेविरोरुजुअलासु मउलिअच्छीसु लुलिअचिहुरासु पुरिसाइरीसु कामो पिआसु सज्जाउहो होइ
english:
The God of Love strings his bow
When women sit astride their husbands,
Thighs twitching,
Eyes closing
And hair tossing around.

prakrit: जं जं ते ण सुहाअइ तं तं ण करेमि जं मम्ऽ आअत्तं अहअं चिअ जं ण सुहामि सुहअ तं किं मम्ऽ आअत्तं
english:
But, my dear,
What can I do about the things
I don’t like?

prakrit: वावारविसंवाअं सअलावअवाण कुणइ हअलज्जा सवणाण उणो गुरुसंणिहे वि ण णिरुंझइ णिओअं
english:
Wretched shyness stops her every limb
From acting naturally,
But even with her parents standing by
It cannot force her not to use her ears.

prakrit: किं भणह मं सहीओ मा मर दीसिहइ सो जिअंतीए कज्जालाओ एसो सिणेहमग्गो उण ण होइ
english:
“Don’t die, only keep alive
And you’ll see him once again”?
That may be practical advice
But it’s not the way of love.

prakrit: एक्कल्लमओ दिट्ठीअ मइअ तह पुलइओ सअण्हाए पिअजाअस्स जह धणुं पडिअं वाहस्स हत्थाओ
english:
The doe looked at the solitary stag
With such longing
That the bow dropped from the hand
Of the hunter
Whose wife was dear to him.

prakrit: णलिणीसु भमसि परिमलसि सत्तलं मालइं पि णो मुअसि तरलत्तणं तुह अहो महुअर जइ पाडला हरइ
english:
O bee,
One moment you hover above the lotuses,
The next you brush against the mimosa,
Then you stay glued to the jasmine.
Perhaps the trumpetflowers will cure you
Of this fickleness.

prakrit: दोअंगुलअकवालअपिणद्धसविसेसणीलकंचुइआ दावेइ थणत्थलवण्णिअं व तरुणी जुअजणाणं
english:
The girl has left open her dark blue bodice,
Like a door two inches ajar,
To show the young men
A sample of her breasts.

prakrit: रक्खेइ पुत्तअं मत्थएण ओच्छोअअं पडिच्छंती अंसूहि पहिअघरिणी ओल्लिज्जंतं ण लक्खेइ
english:
With her head catching the water
That drips from the eaves
She protects her little son,
But does not notice that he is wet
With the tears she sheds
For her absent husband.

prakrit: अब्भंतरसरसाओ उवरिं पव्वाअवद्धपंकाओ चंकम्मंतम्मि जणे समूससंति व्व रच्छाओ
english:
With a dry crust of mud on the surface
But still wet below
The village streets seem to sigh heavily
As people go to and fro.

prakrit: मुहपुंडरीअछाआइ संठिए उअह राअहंसे व्व छणपिट्ठकुट्टणुच्छलिअधूलिधवले थणे वहइ
english:
Look at her breasts
Powdered with the flour
She grinds for the festival,
Like two white geese sitting in the shade
Of the lotus of her face.

prakrit: तह तेण वि सा दिट्ठा तीअ वि तह तस्स पेसिआ दिट्ठी जह दोण्ह वि समअं चिअ णिव्वुत्तरआइ जाआइ
english:
He looked at her
In such a way,
And she at him,
That, at the same moment,
They both consummated their love.

prakrit: दुस्सिक्खिअरअणपसिक्खएहि घिट्ठो सि पत्थरे ताव जा तिलमेत्तं वट्टसि मरगअ का तुज्झ मुल्लकहा
english:
O emerald,
A succession of incompetent jewelers
Has ground you so long on the stone
That you are no bigger than a sesame seed.
So what’s this talk about being valuable?

prakrit: जह चिंतेइ परिअणो आसंकइ जह अ तस्स पडिवक्खो वालेण वि गामणिणंदणेण तह रक्खिआ पल्ली
english:
As his relatives suspected
And his enemies feared,
The headman’s son,
Though still very young,
Has proved excellent
At protecting the village.

prakrit: अण्णेसु पहिअ पुच्छसु वाहअपुत्तेसु पुसिअचम्माइं अम्हं वाहजुआणो हरिणेसु धणुं ण णामेइ
english:
Trader,
For hides of the spotted deer
You’ll have to go elsewhere.
Our young hunter
No longer bends his bow
To shoot deer.

prakrit: गअवहुवेहव्वअरो पुत्तो मे एक्ककंडविणिवाई तह सोण्हाइ पुलइओ जह कंडवरंडअं वहइ
english:
Once he needed just one arrow
To make an elephant cow a widow,
Now he carries a quiverful.

prakrit: अप्पाहेइ मरंतो पुत्तं पल्लीवई पअत्तेण मह णामेण जह तुमं ण लज्जसे तह करेज्जासु
english:
On his deathbed
The headman of the hamlet
Earnestly instructs his son:
“Act in such a way
As not to be ashamed of my name.”

prakrit: अणुमरणपत्थिआए पच्चागअजीविए पिअअमम्मि वेहव्वमंडणं कुलवहूअ सोहग्गअं जाअं
english:
As the good wife prepared
To join her beloved on the pyre
He came back to life,
And her widow’s finery
Turned to that of married bliss.

prakrit: महुमच्छिआइ दट्ठं दट्ठूण मुहं पिअस्स सूणोट्ठं ईसालुई पुलिंदी रुक्खच्छाअं गआ अण्णं
english:
When the Pulinda hunter’s wife
Saw her husband’s lip
Swollen by a bee sting,
She was stung herself
By jealousy
And fled
To stand in the shade of another tree.

prakrit: धण्णा वसंति णीसंकमोहणे वहलपत्तलवइम्मि वाअंदोलणओणविअवेणुगहणे गिरिग्गामे
english:
Lucky are they who live
In a mountain village
Where one can make love
Undisturbed
Beneath a leafy hedge
Or in deep thickets of reeds
That bend and sway in the wind.

prakrit: तह परिमलिआ गोवेण तेण हत्थं पि जा ण ओल्लेइ स च्चिअ खडणा एण्हिं पेच्छह कुडदोहिणी जाआ
english:
However much he squeezed her teats—
Look how she’s now yielding milk
By the pail.

prakrit: धवलो जिअइ तुह कए धवलस्स कए जिअंति गिट्ठीओ जिअ तंवे अम्ह वि जीविएण गोट्ठं तुमाअत्तं
english:
The bull lives only for you
And the heifers only for the bull,
So please, dear cow, stay alive
For the life of our herd
Depends on your life.

prakrit: अग्घाइ छिवइ चुंवइ ठवेइ हिअअम्मि जणिअरोमंचो जाआकवोलसरिसं पेच्छह पहिओ महुअपुप्फं
english:
Look how the traveler thrills
As he sniffs at the sweetwood flower,
Caresses it, kisses it,
And presses it against his heart
As if it were his wife’s pale cheek.

prakrit: उअ ओल्लिज्जइ मोहं भुअंगकित्तीअ कडअलग्गाइ ओज्झरधारासद्धालुएण सीसं वणगएण
english:
See how the elephant tries in vain
To wet its head beneath a snake’s sloughed skin
That hangs down from a rocky sill,
Mistaking it for a waterfall.

prakrit: कमलं मुअंत महुअर पिक्ककइत्थाण गंधलोहेण आलेक्खलड्डुअं पामरो व्व छिविऊण जाणिहिसि
english:
To desert the lotus
For the scent of ripe wood-apple—
As soon as you touch it, silly bee,
You’ll find out your mistake,
Like the yokel
Who reached for a sweetmeat in a painting.

prakrit: गिज्जंते मंगलगाइआहि वरगोत्तदिण्णअण्णाए सोउं व णिग्गओ उअह होंतवहुआइ रोमंचो
english:
Look,
When the women start singing the wedding songs
Goose pimples break out on the bride-to-be
As if they too want to hear
The bridegroom’s name.

prakrit: मण्णे आअण्णंता आसण्णविवाहमंगलुग्गीअं तेहि जुआणेहि समं हसंति मं वेअसकुडुंगा
english:
When her relatives whirled around the sheet
Said to be stained with the young bride’s blood
The virgin’s lover looked on with a glint in his eye. 
I suspect the reed beds by the river
As well as the young men
Had a good laugh when they heard
The solemn song of blessing
On the eve of my wedding.

prakrit: ण अ दिट्ठिं णेइ मुहं ण अ छिविउं देइ णा लवइ किं पि तह वि हु किं पि रहस्सं णववहुसंगो पिओ होइ
english:
She does not look at you,
Does not allow you to touch her face,
Says nothing.
Yet,
In some mysterious way,
Being with a young bride is pure joy.

prakrit: अलिअपसुत्तवलंतम्मि णववरे णववहूअ वेवंतो संवेल्लिओरुसंजमिअवत्थगंठिं गओ हत्थो
english:
Feigning sleep
The husband turned over
And let a trembling hand fall as if by accident
On the knot of his young wife’s skirt
Which she held firmly between her thighs.

prakrit: पुच्छिज्जंती ण भणइ गहिआ पप्फुरइ चुंविआ रुअइ तुण्हिक्का णववहुआ कआवराहेण उवऊढा
english:
When he asks her a question
She does not reply,
When he touches her
She recoils,
When he kisses her
She starts to cry,
And when he attempts an embrace
His young wife’s silence
Makes him feel like a criminal.

prakrit: तत्तो च्चिअ होंति कहा विअसंति तहिं तहिं समप्पंति किं मण्णे माउच्छा एक्कजुआणो इमो गामो
english:
He seems to be
The beginning, middle and end
Of every conversation.
Am I to suppose, dear aunt,
That in this entire village
There is only one young man?

prakrit: जाइ वअणाइ अम्हे वि जंपिमो ताइ जंपइ जणो वि ताइं चिअ तेण पजंपिआइ हिअअं सुहावेंति
english:
The things we say
That are commonplace,
When he says them,
Fill the heart with joy.

prakrit: सव्वाअरेण मग्गह पिअं जणं जइ सुहेण वो कज्जं जं जस्स हिअअदइअं तं ण सुहं जं तहिं ण त्थि
english:
If you want to be happy
Take care in choosing a lover,
For what is dear to you
Does not bring happiness
Unless you are dear to it.

prakrit: दीसंतो दिट्ठिसुहो चिंतिज्जंत मणवल्लहो अत्ता उल्लावंत सुइसुहो पिओ जणो णिच्च रमणिज्जो
english:
When one sees him
He gives satisfaction to the eyes,
When one thinks of him
He gives satisfaction to the mind,
When he speaks
He gives satisfaction to the ears:
Dear mother-in-law,
My husband is always delightful. 
Going over everything you do,
Everything you say,
Every trick of your eyes
—One day is too short.

prakrit: ठाणब्भट्ठा परिगलिअपीणआ उण्णईअ परिचत्ता अम्हे उण ठेरिपओहर व्व उअरे च्चिअ णिसन्णा
english:
Fallen from our former position,
Our abundance wasted away,
No longer prominent,
We stare at our navels
Like an old woman’s breasts.

prakrit: पच्चूसागअ रंजिअदेह पिआलोअ लोअणाणंद अण्णत्तखविअसव्वरि णहभूसण दिणवइ णमो दे
english:
What a feast for the eyes,
When you return at dawn
Red all over
Having spent the night elsewhere.
Ornament of the sky,
Lord of the day,
We bow before you humbly.

prakrit: विवरीअसुरअलेहड पुच्छसि मह कीस गब्भसंभूइं ओअत्ते कुंभमुहे जललवकणिआ वि किं ठाइ
english:
You who like to make love upside down,
How can you ask if I am pregnant?
Is there a single drop of water left
In a jug turned upside down?

prakrit: अच्चासण्णविवाहे समं जसोआइ तरुणगोवीहिं वड्ढंते महुमहणे संवंधा णिण्हुविज्जंति
english:
The young cowgirls tried to conceal their family ties
With his mother Yaóoda\.

prakrit: जं जं आलिहइ मणो आसावत्तीहि हिअअफलअम्मि तं तं वालो व्व विही णिहुअं हसिऊण पम्हुसइ
english:
Fate, like a naughty child,
Wipes out with a secret smile.

prakrit: दूरंतरिए वि पिए कह वि णिअत्ताइ मज्झ णअणाइ हिअअं उण तेण समं अज्ज वि अणिवारिअं भमइ
english:
When my lover left for distant lands
My eyes found their way back home,
But my heart is still beside him
Going wherever he goes.

prakrit: तस्स कहाकंतइए सद्दाअण्णणसमोसरिअकोवे समुहालोअणकंपिरि उवऊढा किं पवज्जिहिसि
english:
Seeing him face to face, you tremble.
What will you do when you embrace?

prakrit: भरणमिअणीलसाहग्गखलिअचलणद्धविहुअवक्खउडा तरुसिहरेसु विहंगा कह कह वि लहंति संठाणं
english:
With great difficulty
Birds find a place to rest
In the treetops,
Fluttering their wings
And shifting their feet
At the tips of the dark branches
That bend beneath their weight.

prakrit: खाणेण अ पाणेण अ तह गहिओ मंडलो अडअणाइ जह जारं अहिणंदइ भुक्कइ घरसामिए एंते
english:
To welcome her lover
And bark at her husband as he comes home.

prakrit: कंडंतेण अकंडं पल्लीमज्झम्मि विअडकोअंदं पइमरणाहि वि अहिअं वाहेण रुआविआ अत्ता
english:
When our hunter
In the middle of the village, for everybody to see,
Started to cut sections into his gruesome bow
Mother-in-law began to cry
Louder even than on the day her husband died.

prakrit: अम्हे उज्जुअसीला पिओ वि पिअसहि विआरपरिओसो ण हु अण्णा का वि गई वाहोहा कह पुसिज्जंतु
english:
My dear,
I am a simple person,
While my lover only likes fancy tricks.
Tell me, is there some other way of wiping my eyes?

prakrit: चंचुपुडाहअविअलिअसहआररसेण सित्तदेहस्स कीरस्स मग्गलग्गं गंधंधं भमइ भमरउलं
english:
A swarm of bees blindly pursues the parrot
Whose feathers are spattered with the juice
Dripping from the mango it has pecked with its beak.

prakrit: एत्थ णिमज्जइ एत्ता एत्थ अहं एत्थ परिअणो सअलो पंथिअ रत्तीअंधअ मा मह सअणे णिमज्जिहिसि
english:
Here’s my mother-in-law’s bed,
Here is mine,
And there those of the servants.
Traveler, you won’t see in the dark,
So do be careful not to fall into mine.

prakrit: परिओससुंदराइं सुरएसु लहंति जाइ सोक्खाइ ताइं चिअ उण विरहे खाउग्गिण्णाइ कीरंति
english:
The pleasures of love,
Which gave the women such complete delight,
Are chewed over and endlessly regurgitated
Once their husbands have gone abroad.

prakrit: मग्गं चिअ अलहंतो हारो पीणुण्णआण थणआण उव्विग्गो भमइ उरे जमुणाणइफेणपुंजो व्व
english:
Finding no way between her full, upcurving breasts,
Her necklace of pearls dances upon her chest
Like the bubbles of foam on the Yamuna\.

prakrit: जे जे गुणिणो जे जे अ चाइणो जे विअड्ढविण्णाणा दारिद्द रे विअक्खण ताण तुमं साणुराओ सि
english:
All the worthy people I meet,
All the generous and discerning ones
Already receive your favors,
You clever devil,
Poverty.

prakrit: जइ कोत्तिओ सि सुंदर सअलतिहीचंददंसणसुहाणं ता मसिणं मोइज्जंतकंचुअं पेक्खसु मुहं से
english:
Handsome man,
If you want to enjoy the sight
Of every phase of the moon
Look at her gentle face
As she slowly lifts her bodice
Over her head.

prakrit: समविसमणिव्विसेसा समंतओ मंदमंदसंचारा अइरा होहिंति पहा मणोरहाणं पि दुल्लंघा
english:
The lie of the land is blurred
And everywhere progress gets slower:
Soon the roads will be impassable
Even for the wheels of his fancy.

prakrit: अइदीहराइ वहुए सीसे दीसंति वंसवत्ताइ भणिए भणामि अत्ता तुम्हाण वि पंडरा पुट्ठी
english:
When she said:
“Daughter-in-law,
There are long bamboo leaves in your hair.”
I said:
“Mother-in-law,
Your back is all white.”

prakrit: अत्थक्करूसणं खणपसिज्जणं अलिअवअणणिव्वंधो उम्मच्छरसंतावो पुत्तअ पअवी सिणेहस्स
english:
Sudden outbursts of anger and the next moment calm,
Constant pretence as well as agonies of jealousy,
Such, my son, is the nature of love.

prakrit: अइ उज्जुए ण लज्जसि पुच्छिज्जंती पिअस्स चरिआइं सव्वंगसुरहिणो मरुवअस्स किं कुसुमरिद्धीहि
english:
You simpleton,
Shouldn’t you be a little more discreet
When people ask how your lover behaves?
With all its leaves so fragrant,
What need has the marjoram for a mass of blooms?

prakrit: मुद्धे अपत्तिअंती पवालअंकुरअणिद्धलोहिअए णिद्धोअधाउराए कीस सहत्थे पुणो धुअसि
english:
Foolish girl,
Why are you still washing
The red powder from your soft palms
That are by nature pink
As a spray of coral?

prakrit: पुससु मुहं ता पुत्तिअ वाहोअरणं विसेसरमणिज्जं मा एअं चिअ मुहमंडणं ति सो काहिइ पुणो वि
english:
Young girl,
Wipe away these tears
Which make your face look even more attractive,
For he might do it again,
Thinking how well they suit you.

prakrit: अवरण्हागअजामाउअस्स विउणेइ मोहणुक्कंठं वहुआइ घरपलोहरमज्जणपिसुणो वलअसद्दो
english:
The tinkle of bracelets
As she took a bath in the backyard of her parents’ house
Redoubled the passion of her husband
Who had arrived in the late afternoon.

prakrit: जुज्झचवेडामोडिअजज्जरकण्णस्स जुण्णमल्लस्स कच्छावंधो च्चिअ भीरुमल्लहिअअं समुक्खणइ
english:
The old wrestler
With ears mangled by many a fight
Has only to gird up his loins
For his fearful opponent to lose heart.

prakrit: आणंदंतेन तुमं पइणो पहएण पडहसद्देण मल्लि ण लज्जसि णच्चसि दोहग्गे पाअडिज्जंते
english:
Instead of dancing for joy
Should you not feel shame?
The drum that celebrates your husband’s victory
In the wrestling match
Makes your unhappy marriage
Known to all the world.

prakrit: मा वच्चह वीसंभं इमाण वहुचाडुअम्मणिउणाणं णिव्वत्तिअकज्जपरम्मुहाण सुणआण व खलाणं
english:
But once they’ve had their way
They turn their backs on you.

prakrit: सच्चं साहसु देअर तह तह चडुआरएण सुणएण णिव्वत्तिअकज्जपरम्मुहत्तणं सिक्खिअं कत्तो
english:
Tell me, brother-in-law,
That dog who fawned on the bitch
So persistently,
Where did he learn to turn his back on her
The moment he’d had his way?

prakrit: णिप्पण्णसस्सरिद्धी सच्छंदं गाइ पामरो सरए दलिअणवसालितंडुलधवलमिअंकासु राईसु
english:
In autumn, when the corn is ripe,
The farmer would do as he likes
If only the moonlit nights weren’t bright
As the fresh grains of husked rice.

prakrit: अलिहिज्जइ पंकअले हलालिचलणेण कलमगोवीए केआरसोअरुंभणतंसट्ठिअकोमलो चलणो
english:
Bees circle round the rice girl’s pink foot
Placed across the sluice
To stop the water flowing
Into the paddy field.

prakrit: दिअहे दिअहे सूसइ संकेअअभंगवड्ढिआसंका आवंडुरोणअमुही कलमेण समं कलमगोवी
english:
Just like the rice,
Each day her head bends lower
And her face grows paler
—The girl who guards the paddy,
As she grows more fearful of the day
When her occupation will cease.

prakrit: दट्ठूण हरिअदीहं गोसे संढाण जूरए हलिओ असईरहस्समग्गं तुसारधवले तिलच्छेत्ते
english:
When at dawn he sees a long swathe of green
Through the frost-white field of sesame
—His faithless wife’s clandestine trail—
The farmer blames himself for letting loose the bulls. 
Seeing his wife’s dark breasts
Which, but for the absence of smoke,

prakrit: संकेल्लिओ व्व णिज्जइ खंडं खंडं कओ व्व पीओ व्व वासागमम्मि मग्गो घरहुत्तमुहेण पहिएण
english:
At the approach of the rains
The traveler is so intent on getting home
That he bundles up the road and takes it with him,
That he cuts it into manageable pieces,
That he swallows it down in one gulp.

prakrit: एण्हिं वारेइ जणो तइआ मूइल्लओ कहिं व गओ जाहे विसं व जाअं सव्वंगपहोलिरं पेम्मं
english:
Nowadays people restrain me
But when this love,
Which like a poison courses through all my limbs,
First began
They were mute.

prakrit: अण्णोण्णकलक्खंतरपेसिअमेलीणदिट्ठिपसराइं दो च्चिअ मण्णे कअभंडणाइ समअं पहसिआइं
english:
When their surreptitious glances met
The couple whom I thought were sulking
Both burst out laughing.

prakrit: धण्णा वहिरंधरआ ते च्चिअ जीअंति माणुसे लोए ण सुणंति पिसुणवअणं खलाण अद्धिं ण पेक्खंति
english:
Happy are the deaf and the blind!
Though they live among humankind
They hear not a single word of slander
Nor see how the wicked prosper.

prakrit: गामारुह म्हि गामे वसामि णअरट्ठिइं ण आणामि णाअरिआणं पइणो हरेमि जा होमि सा होमि
english:
I was born and raised in a village,
I live in a village,
And with the ways of the town
I am not familiar.
Still,
I attract the husbands of the women in town.
I am what I am.

prakrit: ओसर रोत्तुं चिअ णिम्मिआइ मा पुससु मे हअच्छीइं दंसणमेत्तुम्मइएहि जेहि सीलं तुह ण णाअं
english:
Be off!
Stop wiping away my tears!
Crying is what my stupid eyes were made for.
For when they tried looking they got distracted
And failed to see your true nature.

prakrit: रणरणअसुण्णहिअओ चिंतंतो विरहदुव्वलं जाअं अमुणिअणिअवसही सो वोलीणो गाममज्झेण
english:
He was so distracted with worry
About his wife not surviving the long separation
That, reaching his house,
He walked on.

prakrit: सुअणो णि दीसइ च्चिअ खलबहुल डड्ढजीअलोअम्मि जह काअसंकुला तह ण हंसपरिवारिआ पुहवी
english:
In this damned world of ours, so full of crooks,
A decent man is rarely seen.
The earth teems with crows
But geese are few and far between.

prakrit: जं मुच्छिआइ ण सुओ कलंवगंधेण तं गुण पडिअं इअरा गज्जिअसद्दो जीएण विणा ण वोलंतो
english:
That she did not hear the thunder
Because she was swooning from the scent
Of the kadamba flower
Saved her life.
Otherwise the thunder would not have passed by
Without taking it.

prakrit: जं असरणो व्व डड्ढो गामो साहीणवहुजुआणो वि संभमविसंठुलाणं तं दुच्चरिअं तुह थणाणं
english:
That our village burnt down
As though there were no help for it,
Despite the number of young men at hand,
Is the doing of your wicked breasts
Which in the confusion
Were swaying about.

prakrit: अह सुअइ दिण्णपडिवक्खवेअणा पसिढिलेहि अंगेहिं णिव्वत्तिअसुरअरसाणुवंधसुहणिब्भरं वहुआ
english:
After tasting to the full the pleasures of love
The young wife slept soundly,
Unaware how her weary limbs
Were a torture to her rivals.

prakrit: जइ तेण तुज्झ वअणं ण कअं मह कारणेण अ हआसे सा कीस खंडिअतडं णिआहरं दूइ दुम्मेसि
english:
If he does not do what you ask him
On my behalf, you useless messenger,
Why not give your lips a rest?
They are already bruised.

prakrit: दिट्ठाइ जं ण दिट्ठो सरलसहावाइ जं च णा ऽलविओ उवआरो जं ण कओ तं चिअ कलिअं छइल्लेहिं
english:
That you did not look at him
When he looked at you,
That you did not speak to him
While you are usually so forthright,
That you did not want to show him kindness
—Our clever friends have noted it all.

prakrit: अविरलणिग्गअपुलओ पअडिअकंपो पमुक्कसिक्कारो हेमंते पहिअजणो सुरआसत्तो व्व पडिहाइ
english:
Covered all over with gooseflesh,
Visibly shivering,
Sucking in his breath between clenched teeth:
The winter wayfarer
Looks like a man making love.

prakrit: सेउल्लणिअंवालग्गसण्हसिचअस्स मग्गम् अलहंतो सहि मोहघोलिरो अज्ज तस्स हसिओ मए हत्थो
english:
Friend,
I had to laugh
When his hand fumbled at my thin skirt,
Which stuck tight to my sweating thighs.

prakrit: अमिअमअं चिअ हिअअं हत्था तण्हाहरा सअण्हाणं चंदमुहि कत्थ णिवसइ अमित्तदहणो तुह पआवो
english:
Your heart is made of nectar,
Your hands allay the yearning of those who yearn,
Where, O moon-faced girl,
Do you produce the heat
With which you burn your enemies?

prakrit: दिट्ठीअ जाव पसरो ताव तुमं सुहअ णिव्वुइं कुणसि वोलीणदंसणो तह तवेसि जह होउदिट्ठेण
english:
As long as you are within sight
My beautiful man, you give pleasure.
But as soon as you are out of sight
You cause such grief
That I never want to see you again.

prakrit: गज्जंति घणा पंथाणो वहुतणा अ पसारिआ सरिआ अज्ज वि उज्जुअसीले पइणो मग्गं पलोएसि
english:
Foolish girl!
The clouds thunder,
The paths are grass-grown,
The rivers are swollen,
And still you look down the road
For your husband.

prakrit: उण्हो त्ति समत्थिज्जइ डाहेण सरोरुहाण हेमंतो चरिएहि णज्जइ जणो संगोवंतो वि अप्पाणं
english:
Lotuses know that winter is hot
Because it makes them wilt.
However much they try to hide their true nature,
People are betrayed by their acts.

prakrit: उवहारिआइ समअं पिंडारे उअ कहं कुणंतम्मि णववहुआइ सरोसं सव्व च्चिअ वछआ मुक्का
english:
Look!
While the cowherd is chatting up the maid
His young wife angrily sets all the calves free.

prakrit: पज्जालिऊण अग्गिं मुहेण पुत्तिअ किणो समोसरसि थणअलसपडिअपडिमा फुरंति ण छिवंति ते जाला
english:
You silly girl,
Why do you shrink away each time you blow into the fire?
These flames reflected on your round breasts
Flicker but will not touch you.

prakrit: अग्गिं अब्भुत्तंतीअ पुत्ति पडिमागआ कवोलम्मि कण्णालंविअपल्लवलच्छिं संधेइ ते जाला
english:
Dear girl,
The flames reflected on your cheeks
When you kindle the kitchen fire
Resemble blossoming twigs
Hanging down from your ears.

prakrit: कह दे धूमंधारे अब्भुत्तणं अग्गिणो समप्पिहइ मुहकमलचुंवणालेहडम्मि पासट्ठिए दिअरे
english:
With your young brother-in-law at your side
Trying to kiss the lotus of your mouth?

prakrit: आअंवच्छं पअलंतवाहम् आवद्धथणहरुक्कंपं असमत्तं चिअ चिट्ठउ सिहिणो अब्भुत्तणम् इणं ते
english:
Look at you:
Your eyes are red,
Tears flow down your cheeks,
And your heavy breasts are shaking.
You’d better stop
Trying to kindle the kitchen fire.

prakrit: जइ वि हु दिल्लिंदिलिआ तह वि हु मा पुत्ति णग्गिआ भमसु छेआ णअरजुआणो माअं धूआइ लक्खंति
english:
Little girl,
You can still play bunny hops
But don’t go walking around naked:
The youths of the village
Know the mother by the daughter.

prakrit: गअगंडअगवअसरभसेरिहसद्दूलरिक्खजाईणं थणआ वाहवहूए अभअं दाउं व णिक्कंता
english:
The breasts of the huntsman’s wife rise
As if to tell the elephant, rhinoceros and ox,
The deer, buffalo, tiger and bear
That from her husband
They have nothing to fear.

prakrit: भिउडीअ पुलोइस्सं णिब्भच्छिस्सं परंमुही होस्सं जं भणह तं करिस्सं सहीउ जइ तं ण पेच्छिस्सं
english:
I’ll frown at him,
I’ll abuse him,
And I’ll turn my back on him.
I’ll do whatever you say, friends,
As long as I don’t have to look at him.

prakrit: जं केअवेण पेम्मं जं च वला जं च अत्थलोहेण जं उवरोहणिमित्तं णमोणमो तस्स पेम्मस्स
english:
Love based on pretence,
Love based on force,
Love based on greed,
Love based on compliance
—Honor to them all!

prakrit: कस्स ण सद्धा गरुअत्तणम्मि पइणो पसाअमाणस्स जइ माणभंजणीओ ण होंति हेमंतराईओ
english:
Calm down.
Who’s the angry one?
You, my slim beauty.
How can one be angry with a stranger?
Who’s the stranger?
You, my lord.
How come?
I must have done something wrong in a past life. 
Who would not love to be tough on a cringing husband
But for the long winter nights
That erode one’s pride?

prakrit: अव्वो तहिंतहिं चिअ गअणे भमिऊण वीसमंतेण वोहित्तवाअसेण व्व हासिआ डड्ढपेम्मेण
english:
O! What a fool
Love has made of me,
As when the ship’s crow
Circles the whole heaven
Only to settle on the ship again.

prakrit: दे आ दुमसु तुमं चिय मा परिहर पुत्ति पढमदुमियं ति किं कुड्डं णिअमुहअंदकंतिदुमिअं ण लक्खेसि
english:
Hey there, daughter—
Keep on whitewashing that wall.
Don’t stop because you think the job’s already done.
Can’t you see? It’s the moonlight of your face
That makes it look so white.

prakrit: विज्जंति तणुं उवणेंति वेअणं णेयताण खयमग्गे अब्बो अइट्ठपुव्वो अणंगबाणाण माहप्पो
english:
They pierce one’s body,
Cause pain
But without making wounds.
Ah! Love’s arrows are unique.

prakrit: आमोडऊण बलाउ हत्थं मज्झं गओ सि भो पहिअ हिअआउ जइ अ णीहसि सामत्थं तुज्झ जाणिस्सं
english:
Having wrenched yourself from my grip
You left for the road,
But only when you manage to escape from my heart as well
Will I acknowledge your power.

prakrit: सद्धा मे तुज्झ पियत्तणस्स अहयं तु तं ण याणामि दे पसिय तुमं चिय सिक्खवेसु जह ते पिया होमि
english:
What I do not know
And what you must teach me, please,
Is how you will come to love me.

prakrit: पेम्मुम्मइयाइ मए उवऊढो हलियउत्तबुद्धीए फंसेमि जाव फरुसो तणपुरिसो गामसीमाए
english:
Blind with love
I threw my arms around him,
Taking him for a farmer’s boy.
But when I touched him
He was just a spiky scarecrow
At the village boundary.

prakrit: बे मग्गा धरणियले माणिणि माणोण्णयाण पुरिसाण अहवा पावंति सिरिं अहव भमंता समप्पंति
english:
To people who are proud and haughty:
Either they find a treasure
Or they are pilgrims of love
For the rest of their lives.

prakrit: कत्तो कमलाण रई कत्तो कुमुयाण सीअलो चंदो तह सज्जणाण णेहो ण चलइ दूरट्ठिआणं पि
english:
What has the hot sun,
Which makes the lotuses unfold,
In common with the cool moon
Which makes the water lilies unfold?
It’s as with a good man’s affection,
Which does not alter
However far away he may be.

prakrit: हा हा किं तेण कयं मालइविरहम्मि पुत्ति भसलेणं कंकेल्लिकुसुममज्झे जलणो त्ति समप्पिओ अप्पा
english:
Do you know, my daughter,
What the poor bee did
In the absence of the jasmine flower?
He threw himself into the red aóoka flowers
As into a fire.

prakrit: ढंखरसेसो वि हु महुअरेण मुक्को ण मालईविडवो दरवियसियकलियामोयबहलिमं संभरंतेण
english:
Though the twigs were completely bare,
The bees clung to the jasmine,
Savoring the memory of the scent
Which burst from the buds that had been there.

prakrit: डहिऊण सयलरण्णं अग्गी समविसमलंघणुव्वायो तडलंबंततणेहिं तिसिय व्व णइं समोसरइ
english:
After burning the entire forest
The fire was worn out
With crossing hill and plain.
So, holding on to the grass that grew on the steep bank,
It went down to the river for a drink.

prakrit: स च्चिय रामेउ तुमं पंडिय णिच्चं अलं म्ह रमिएण सब्भावबाहिराइं जा जाणइ अट्टमट्टाइं
english:
If you are the scholar
You say you are,
Then go to her
And stop bothering us,
For she knows all the tricks in the book.

prakrit: रअणायरस्स साहेमि णम्मए अज्ज मुक्कदक्खिण्णा वेडिसलयाहरंतेण मिलिया जं सि पूरेण
english:
River-nymph Narmada\,
Today I will put discretion aside
And tell your husband, the sea,
How you had a rendezvous with the stream
In a bower of reeds.

prakrit: तिसिया पियउ त्ति मओ मओ वि तिसिओ मई करेऊण इय मयमिहुणं तिसियं पियइ ण सलिलं सिणेहेण
english:
The stag thought that she was more thirsty than he
And the doe thought that he was more thirsty than she.
In this way the thirsty couple
Out of consideration for each other
Did not drink at all!

prakrit: णिवडिहिसि सुण्णहियए जलहरजलपंकिलम्मि मग्गम्मि उप्पेक्खागयपिययमहत्थे हत्थं पसारेंती
english:
Watch out!
The path is slippery after today’s shower.
If you grope for the hand of your lover
Whom you imagine is standing beside you,
You are bound to fall over.

prakrit: उच्छंगियाĕ पइणा अहिसारणपंकमलिणपेरंते आसण्णपरियणो विअ सेअ च्चिय धुवइ से पाए
english:
As soon as her husband took her on his lap
Sweat poured from her
Like an attentive servant,
Washing the mud of last night’s assignation
From her feet.

prakrit: जह लंघेसि परवइं निययवइं भरसहं पि मोत्तूणं तह मण्णे कोहलिए अज्जं कल्लं पि फुट्टिहसि
english:
—One of these days, I tell you,
You will fall and burst open.

prakrit: अणुसोयइ हलियबहू रइकिरणोलुग्गपंडुरच्छायं रण्णुंदुरदंतुक्खुत्तविसमवलियं तिलच्छेत्तं
english:
The plowman’s wife bemoans the sesame field,
Bleached by the rays of the sun
And pockmarked by the burrowing of field mice.

prakrit: किं तस्स पारएणं किम् अग्गिणा किं च गब्भहरएण जस्स णिसम्मइ उअरे उण्हायंतत्थणी जाया
english:
Who needs a blanket,
Who needs a fire,
And who needs a bedchamber,
If he has a wife with warm breasts
Lying snug against his chest?

prakrit: कमलायराण उण्हो हेमंतो सीअलो जणवयस्स को किर भिण्णसहावं जाणइ परमत्थयं लोए
english:
Winter,
Which is cool for people
Wilts the lotuses.
How can we truly know the world
When it varies according to circumstance?

prakrit: पढमं चिय माहवपट्टयं व घेत्तूण डाहिणो वाओ अंकोल्लपढमवत्तं पहिंडिओ गामरच्छासु
english:
As though seizing the first sign of spring,
The south wind whirls the first am≥kolla leaf
Through the village streets.

prakrit: सो माणो पियमुहअंददंसणे कह थिरो धरिज्जिहइ अंकोल्लकोरआण वि जो फुट्टमुहाण बीहेइ
english:
How is this pride going to stand its ground
At the sight of the beloved’s face
If it is already alarmed
By the burgeoning buds of the am≥kolla?

prakrit: कारणगहिओ वि मए माणो एमेअ जं समोसरियो अत्थक्कफुल्ल अंकोल्ल तुज्झ तं मत्थए पडउ
english:
That my sulking mood,
Which was completely justified,
Disappeared just like that,
Is entirely your doing,
Am≥kolla plant,
As you unexpectedly started to blossom.

prakrit: दूरपइण्णपरिमलं सपल्लवं मुद्धपुप्फपंगुरणं अंगच्छित्तं पिव वम्महेण दिण्णं महुसिरीए
english:
Spring offers her body
With its drifting fragrance,
Leafspray fingers,
And cloak of tender flowers
As a playground
To the God of Love.

prakrit: कारणगहिअं पि इमा माणं मोएइ माणिणिअणस्स सहयारमंजरी पियसहि व्व कण्णे समल्लीणा
english:
This mango bud
Hanging from her ear,
Like a dear friend whispering good advice,
Succeeds in banishing sulkiness,
However justified it may be.

prakrit: अज्जं चिय छणदिअहो मा पुत्ति रुएहि एहइ पिओ त्ति सुण्हं आसासंती पडियत्तमुही रुवइ सासू
english:
“My daughter, stop crying.
He will come.
For he has never before missed today’s fair.”
With these empty words
She reassured her son’s wife,
Turning her face away
To hide her tears.

prakrit: आउच्छणोवऊहणकंठसमोसरियबाहुलइयाए वलयाइ पहियचलणे बहूĕ णियलाइ व पडंति
english:
As the wife embraced her husband
Who was about to depart,
The bracelets slipped from her slender arms
Clasped around his neck
And fell at his feet like shackles.

prakrit: गुणसालिणो वि करिणो होहइ जूहाहिवत्तणं कत्तो णवसालिकवललोहिल्लिआĕ विंझं मुअंतस्स
english:
How can an elephant
Ever become leader of the herd
If he leaves the Vindhya
With its wild rice
For the sake of a mouthful of rice
From a paddy field?

prakrit: णक्खमऊहेसु खणं कुसुमेसु खणं खणं किसलएसु हत्थेसु खणं कुसुमोच्चियाइ लोडाविया भसला
english:
The next over the shoots,
And the next over the girl’s hands.

prakrit: अंतो णिभुअठ्ठिअपरिअणाइ ओरुद्धदारणअणाइ गिम्हे घोरट्टघग्घररवेण घोरंति व घरट्ट
english:
In summer the rattle of the grindstone
Makes it seem that the houses are snoring
While the doors, their eyes, remain shut
And the servants make no stir inside.

prakrit: जीहाइ परं लिब्भइ दंतोट्ठेणं ण तीरए गहिउं अहरो व्व सव्वणो गोहणेण पढमो तणुच्छेओ
english:
The cow merely licks it with its tongue
As one does a bruised lip.

prakrit: विलासणिगुरुणिअंवो तीरउ चलणेहि दोहि उव्वहिउं एआई उण मज्झो थणभारं कइ णु उव्वहि
english:
It takes two feet to support the woman’s broad hips.
So how can her waist on its own
Support her heavy breasts?

prakrit: वारिज्जंती णवकोमुइ त्ति मा पुत्ति अंगणे सुवसु मा ते अंवुपिसाओ चंदो त्ति मुहं गसिज्जिहिइ
english:
Daughter, be warned:
It’s a new moon.
So do not sleep in the courtyard tonight
Lest the moon-eating demon swallow your face by
mistake.

prakrit: सुव्वंतो आसि परंपराइ कहकह वि दिट्ठिमिलिओ ऽसि दे सुहअ किं पि जंपसु पिअंतु कण्णाइ मे अमिअं
english:
I have heard so much about you from others
And now at last I see you with my own eyes.
Please, my dear, say something
So that my ears, too, may drink nectar.

prakrit: विरहकिसिआ वराई दिणाइ आसण्णगिम्हपरिणामाइं कढिणहिअओ पवासी ण आणिमो कह समप्पिहिइ
english:
Left on her own,
The poor woman has grown very thin,
The summer is almost over,
And her hardhearted husband is still abroad:
I don’t know how this will end.

prakrit: रोआविअ म्ह माए अंगणपहिएण दरपसुत्तेण परिवत्तसु माणिणि माणिणि त्ति सिविणे भणंतेण
english:
Mother, if you ask me what made me cry,
It’s that traveler dozing in our courtyard
Who in his dream pleaded: “Stop being angry,
Please stop being angry and turn over.”

prakrit: माणंसिणीअ पइणा णअणकवोलाहरप्पहाभिण्णा उज्जुअसुरचावणिहा वाहोआरा चिरं दिट्ठा
english:
Intently the husband gazed at the stream of tears
First reflecting the color of his angry wife’s eyes,
Then of her cheeks, and then of her lips.
It looked like a rainbow,
Except that it was straight.

prakrit: सरहसविणिग्गआइ वि इच्छाइ तुमं ण तीअ सच्चविओ सीसाहअवलिअभुअंगवंकरच्छे हअग्गामे
english:
Though she ran out quickly
She failed to spot you.
In this damned village the street twists
Like a snake hit on the head.

prakrit: अणुराअरअणभरिअं कंचणकलस व्व तरुणिथणवट्टं तस्स चिअ मुहम्मि किआ मसिमुद्दा मअणराएण
english:
The girl’s breasts
Are like golden pots filled with the jewels of affection.
In the middle of each
King Love has set his dark seal.

prakrit: गाहाण अ गेआण अ तन्तीसद्दाण पोढमहिलाण ताणं सो च्चिअ दण्डो जे ताण रसं ण आणंति
english:
Poems, songs, the sound of the lute,
And impudent women,
To men who have no taste for such things
They are a punishment.

prakrit: लडहविलआण लोअणकडक्खविक्खेवजणिअसंदावा झिज्जंति महासत्ता चित्तुव्वेअणसहा होंति
english:
The pain inflicted by the sidelong glances
Of seductive women
—Saints suffer it too
But they master their emotions.

prakrit: अव्वो ण आमि छेत्तं खज्जउ साली वि कीरणिवहेहिं जाणंता अवि पहिआ पुच्छंति पुणोपुणो मग्गं
english:
I won’t go to the field.
What do I care if the parrots eat all the paddy.
Whenever I’m there
Travelers keep asking me the way
Which they know perfectly well.

prakrit: अत्थक्कागअदिट्ठे वहुआ जामादुअम्मि गुरुपुरओ जूरइ णिवडंताणं हरिसविफंदंतवलआणं
english:
Surprised by her husband’s arrival
At her parents’ house,
The girl was annoyed with her bracelets
For dancing up and down her arms
And threatening to slip off.

prakrit: एह इमीअ णिअच्छह विम्हिअहिअआ सही पुलोएइ अद्दाअम्मि कवोलं कवोलपट्टम्मि अद्दाअं
english:
How surprised the girl is
To see her friend’s cheek in the mirror
And the mirror in her cheek.

prakrit: कइआ जाआ कइआ णु सिक्खिआ माइआ हअकुमारी तंतं जाणइ सव्वं जंजं महिलाŏ जाणंति
english:
How old is she,
This damned girl?
And when did she learn it all?
She already knows everything
That a mature woman knows.

prakrit: खणपिट्ठधूसरत्थणि महुमअतंबच्छि कुवलआभरणे कण्णगअचूअमंजरि पुत्ति तुए मंडिओ गामो
english:
Young girl,
On this day of Holê
—Your breasts dusted with flour,
Your eyes red from too much liquor,
A lotus stuck in your hair
And mango shoots behind your ears—
You are a credit to our village.

prakrit: मग्गिअलद्धे बलमोडिचुंबिए अप्पणेण उवणीदे एक्कम्मि पिआअहरे अण्णण्णा होंति रसहेआ
english:
One time you get them only after a long search,
At another you kiss them after a struggle,
At yet another they are freely offered
—One pair of lover’s lips
Is an inexhaustible source of delights.

prakrit: उप्फुल्ललोअणेणं कवोलबोसट्टमाणसेएणं अहणंतेण वि भणिआ मुहेण से कज्जणिव्वुत्ती
english:
By her wide open eyes,
By the sweat streaming down her cheeks,
The woman said without saying a word
That her part of the business was fulfilled.

prakrit: जत्थ ण उज्जागरओ जत्थ ण ईसा विसूरणं माणो सब्भावचाडुअं जत्थ ण त्थि णेहो तहिं ण त्थि
english:
First let my dear husband come home,
Then let me give him a scolding,
And then let him do his best to bring me round.
There must be someone who can get all three wishes. 
Where there is no wakefulness,
Jealousy, strife, sulking,
And good old flattery,
There no love is either.

prakrit: मह पइणा थणजुअले पत्तं लिहिअं ति गव्विआ कीस आलिहइ महं पि पिओ जइ से कंपो च्चिअ ण होइ
english:
Why so proud
About your husband having painted a pattern
On your breasts?
My husband would have done the same
If his hand had not trembled so much.

prakrit: कण्णे पडिअं हिअए पडिअं चिअ ओ मए अभव्वाए जामि त्ति तुज्झ वअणं किं व ण सहिअं पवासस्स
english:
They fell on my ear
And struck at my heart
—Oh what more can I suffer?—
Your parting words: “I’m off.”

prakrit: समपंथपत्थिअस्स वि पहिअस्स खलंति पुढमदिअहम्मि हिअअट्ठिअजाआगुरुणिअंवहारेण व पआइ
english:
On the very first day of his journey
The traveler stumbled,
Though the path was smooth,
As if hampered by the heavy buttocks
Of the wife he already missed.

prakrit: संदेसो वि ण लिहिओ लेहे पहिएण कत्थ घरतत्ती अणवरअलिहिअगेहिणिगोत्तक्खरपूरिए पत्ते
english:
The traveler had written nothing about himself
Nor asked how things were at home.
The palm leaf was covered all over with the syllables of his
wife’s name
Endlessly repeated.

prakrit: जह दिअहविरामो णवसिरीसगंधुद्धुराणिलग्घविओ पहिअघरिणीअ ण तहा तवेइ तिव्वो वि मज्झण्हो
english:
While her husband was abroad
The fierce midday sun
Did not torment the lonely wife
So much as the evening breeze
Wafting the scent of fresh acacia blooms.

prakrit: चिरपवसिअदइअकहा णिउणाहि सहीहि विरहसहणत्थं अलिआ अवि अवराहा वहूĕ कमसो कहिज्जंति
english:
To help the young wife bear being parted
Her ingenious friends tell her tales
Of lovers’ long absences,
Listing the various ways they cause hurt,
Some quite imaginary.

prakrit: जंजं पउत्थपइआ पिअअमणामक्खरं लिहइ लेहे तंतं तल्लेहणिआणुसारगलिओ पुसइ सेओ
english:
Sweet moon,
Crest jewel of the sky,
Beauty spot on the face of night,
Touch me with the very same rays
With which you’ve touched my most dearly beloved. 
Each time she tries to write a letter
To her husband on his travels
The drops of sweat running down her pen
Blot out the syllables of his dear name.

prakrit: अच्छउ णिमीलिअच्छी मा मा वारेह पिअअमासाए तेण विणा किं पेच्छउ उम्मिल्लेहिं वि अच्छीहिं
english:
Let her sit with eyes closed,
Dreaming of her husband.
For until he returns
What is there for her to look at
With her eyes open?

prakrit: जइ देव्व तुं पसण्णो मा करिहिसि मज्झ माणुसं जम्मं जइ जम्मं मा पेम्मं जइ पेम्मं मा जणे दुलहे
english:
Fate,
Please, don’t let me be born as a human again.
But if it must be,
Then don’t let me fall in love.
But if it must be,
Then don’t let me fall in love
With a man who is so hard to get.

prakrit: राईण भणइ लोओ जा किल गिम्हम्मि होंति मडहाओ मह उण दइएण विणा ण आणिमो कीस वड्ढंति
english:
People say that summer nights are short.
But I can’t understand why they last so long
In the absence of my beloved.

prakrit: एक्के अअणे दिअहा वीए रअणीŏ होंति दीहाओ विरहाअणो अपुव्वो एत्थ दुवे च्चेअ वड्ढंति
english:
For half the year the nights are long,
For the other half the days.
Separation confuses everything
As both its days and nights are long.

prakrit: चिरजीवित्तणकंखिरि मा तम्म रसाअणेहि अथिरेहि विरहं पवज्ज जाअंति जेण जुअदीहरा दिअहा
english:
If you want to live long
Do not trouble about dubious elixirs.
Just arrange for your lover to go away,
Then the days grow long as eons.

prakrit: रुअइ रुअंतीĕ मए ओहिदिणे गणइ झिज्जइ अहं व पिअविरहे मामि सअज्झिआĕ णेहो च्चिअ अपुव्वो
english:
She shares my tears,
Counts off each day,
And grows as thin as I do
While my husband is away.
Aunt, the concern my neighbor shows
Is quite extraordinary.

prakrit: कंठग्गहणेण सअज्झिआĕ अब्भागओवआरेण वहुआĕ पइम्मि वि आगअम्मि सामं मुहं जाअं
english:
As the woman from next door
Threw her arms around him
To welcome him home,
The wife’s face grew dark
Despite her husband’s return.

prakrit: दूई ण एइ चंदो वि उग्गओ जामिणी वि बोलेइ सव्वं सव्वत्थो च्चिअ विसंठुलं कस्स किं भणिमो
english:
The messenger is late,
The moon is high,
And the night is passing.
Everything’s awry
But to whom can I complain?

prakrit: सो णा ऽगओ त्ति पेच्छह परिहासुल्लाविरीĕ दूईए णूमंतीअ पहरिसो ओसट्टइ गंडपासेसु
english:
“He’s not coming,”
Teased the go-between.
But look:
Despite her feigning,
A thrill of excitement
Steals over her face.

prakrit: अप्पाहिआइ तुह तेण जाइ ताइं मए ण मुणिआइ अच्चुण्हस्सासपरिक्खलंतविसमक्खरपआइं
english:
But claiming she hadn’t quite caught them
She made me repeat the message a hundred times. 
What he wanted me to say to you
I didn’t catch
Because he stammered so much
And sighed such burning sighs.

prakrit: साहेंती सहि सुहअं खणेखणे दूमिआ सि मज्झ कए सब्भावणेहकरणिज्जसरिसअं दाव विरइअं तुमए
english:
Addressing my lover again and again.
Dear friend, all this trouble for my sake.
What you do
You seem to do out of true love.

prakrit: जं तुह कज्जं तं चिअ कज्जं मज्झ त्ति जं सआ भणसि ओ दूइ सच्चवअणे अज्ज सि पारं गआ तस्स
english:
You always say: “Your business is my business,”
But today, dear go-between,
You’ve taken it all too literally
And gone too far.

prakrit: णवलअपहरुत्तत्थाĕ तं कअं हलिअवहुआए जं अज्ज वि जुवइजणो घरेघरे सिक्खिउं महइ
english:
Every young maiden
In every household
Would love to be taught.

prakrit: पइपुरओ च्चिअ रभसेण चुंबिओ देवरो अडअणाए मह वअणं मइरागंधिअं ति हाआ तुहं भणइ
english:
Before her husband’s eyes
The brazen woman passionately kissed his brother’s mouth.
She said she did so because he’d said
That her mouth smelt of drink.

prakrit: तह अडअणाĕ रुण्णं पइमरणे बाहरुद्धकंठीए अणुमरणसंकिणो जह जारस्स वि संकिअं हिअअं
english:
The false woman bewailed her dead husband
With such choking sobs
That even her lover was afraid
She might join him on the pyre.

prakrit: महुएहि किं व वालअ हरसि णिअंबाहि जइ वि मे सिअअं साहामि कस्स रण्णे दूरे गामो अहं एक्का
english:
Why waste your time with sweetwood flowers,
You silly?
Just pull the skirt from my hips.
Whom can I call to in this wood?
The village is a long way off
And I am all by myself.

prakrit: अव्वो कालस्स गई सो वि जुआ सरसकव्वदुल्ललिओ पढइ परासरसद्दं अम्हे वि णिअं पइं गमिमो
english:
Oh! the passage of time:
This young man,
Weary of passionate poems,
Now studies law
While we . . .
We stick to our husbands.

prakrit: अवराहसहस्साइं भरिमो हिअएण तम्मि अद्दिट्ठे दिट्ठम्मि उण पिअसही एक्कं पि हु णं ण संभरिमो
english:
When he’s not here
My mind is full of his thousand offenses,
But the moment I see him,
Dear friend,
I can’t think of a single one.

prakrit: भणभण जंजं पडिहाइ तुज्झ तंतं सहामिमो अम्हे असहत्तणं च जीअं च वल्लभे दोइ ण घडंति
english:
Say what you like:
I can take it.
For being touchy
And living with the man one loves
Are two things that don’t go together.

prakrit: एअं चिअ मह णामं भणभण दे सुहअ किं विलक्खो सि पडिहाइ जं ण तुज्झ वि ममं पि किं देण णामेण
english:
Is that to be my name?
Use it, darling, use it:
Don’t be embarrassed.
If my name doesn’t appeal to you
What use is it to me?

prakrit: मा वेलवेसु बहुअं पुत्तअ अलिएहि गोत्तेहिं एसा वि जाणइ च्चिअ परिहासुम्मिस्सभणिआइं
english:
My son, don’t overdo
That joke of calling her by another woman’s name.
Your wife also knows how to be funny.

prakrit: अइ चंडि किं ण पेच्छसि जइ सो वाहरइ अण्णगोत्तेण अह दे इच्छइ मच्छरपणच्चिअच्छं मुहं दट्ठुं
english:
Hey! you angry girl,
Don’t you see
That when he calls you by the wrong name
It’s only because he wants to see your eyes
Rolling with jealousy?

prakrit: वेआरिज्जसि मुद्धे गोत्तक्खलिएहि मा खु तुं रुवसु किं व ण पेच्छइ अण्णह एद्दहमेत्तेहि अच्छीहिं
english:
Foolish girl, you’re in a muddle.
His getting your name wrong
Is nothing to cry about.
What do you think?
With those big eyes
He could never have made such a mistake.

prakrit: एक्कसअणम्मि सुमुही विमुही गरुएण माणबंधेण सिविणकलहम्मि होंती परम्मुही सम्मुही जाआ
english:
Wrapped in her feelings of resentment
She turned her lovely face away
And fell fast asleep on our bed.
But continuing the tiff in dream
She turned away again
And so came face to face with me.

prakrit: ण वि तह तक्खणसुअमण्णुदुक्खविअणाŏ वि रुवंति जह दिट्ठम्मि पिअअमे अणुणिज्जंतीŏ तरुणीŏ
english:
The grief and anger they suffer
At the moment they hear about their husbands’ lapses
Do not make young women cry so loud
As when the husbands appear in person
And ask to be forgiven.

prakrit: हिअए रोसुक्खित्तं पाअपहारं सिरेण पत्थंतो ण हओ दइओ माणंसिणीĕ थोरंसुअं रुण्णं
english:
As her lover begs
To be kicked on the head,
The angry woman
Stops her foot in midair
And sheds big tears.

prakrit: वच्चिहिइ स घरं से लहिहिइ ओआसम् एहिइ सआसं भणिहिइ जं भणिअव्वं पच्चुत्तं किंणु पाविहिइ
english:
She will go to his house,
Then wait her chance,
Then approach him,
Then say what she has to say.
But what will she get for reply?

prakrit: दावंतेण तुह मुहं भुमआभंगम्मि होंतणवसोहं अकएण उवकअं अज्ज मण्णुणा मज्झ पसिअच्छि
english:
Long-eyed girl,
The effect of your anger
Was completely annulled
By the unusual beauty of your face
As you frowned.

prakrit: अइ पीणत्थणउत्थंभिआणणे सुअणु सुणसु मह वअणं अथिरम्मि जुज्जइ ण जोव्वणम्मि माणो पिए कादुं
english:
You there, with the big breasts
Propping your face up,
Pay attention to what I say:
Youth does not last forever.
So don’t look down your nose
At your lover.

prakrit: पडिवक्खस्स वि पुरओ समुहं भणिआ सि तेण पसिअ त्ति अवलंबिअस्स माणिणि माणस्स अ किं फलं अण्णं
english:
What more do you expect
From persisting in your anger?

prakrit: कज्जं विणा वि कअमाणडंबरा पुलअभिण्णसव्वंगी उज्जल्लालिंगणसोक्खलालसा पुत्ति मुणिआ सि
english:
